project.name: aethon
project.description: Aethon is a Tauri 2 + React 19 + TypeScript desktop shell that embeds the pi coding agent and renders its output as live, interactive UI via the A2UI protocol, where the agent dynamically populates the interface rather than a fixed IDE layout. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: agentCrash.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for agent-crash diagnostics selection and running-tool-crash summarization.

nodes.name: agentCrash.ts
nodes.summary: OS-edge subscriber that handles agent-process crashes: selects an actionable diagnostic from the log tail, summarizes interrupted tool calls, fails scheduled tasks, notifies the user, and auto-restarts the agent.

nodes.name: selectAgentCrashDiagnostic
nodes.summary: Scans the agent log tail in reverse to pick the most actionable crash diagnostic line.

nodes.name: summarizeRunningToolCrash
nodes.summary: Summarizes the tool calls that were in flight on a crashed agent tab for the crash notification.

nodes.name: subscribeAgentCrash
nodes.summary: Subscribes to agent-crash events: surfaces a diagnostic, fails scheduled tasks, notifies, and auto-restarts the agent.

nodes.summary: OS-edge subscriber that responds to agent hot-reload events by clearing hang-warning timers and re-bootstrapping the agent process (start, boot layout, report).

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: capText

nodes.name: forgetMemoryEntry
nodes.summary: Removes a memory entry by id or matching text from its scope file under the write lock.

nodes.name: normalize_schedule
nodes.summary: Validates and normalizes a schedule spec (cron/interval/once) before persistence.

nodes.name: parse_cron_field
nodes.summary: Parses a single cron field (ranges, steps, lists, wildcards) into a matcher set.

nodes.name: resolve_loop_prompt
nodes.summary: Resolves the prompt text for a looped scheduled task, applying iteration substitutions.

nodes.name: scheduled_tasks_reconcile_live_tabs
nodes.summary: Reconciles persisted tasks against the frontend's live tab set, failing orphaned runs.

nodes.name: ServerStatus
nodes.summary: Serializable discovery-server status (running flag + bound port).

nodes.name: server_start
nodes.summary: IPC command that boots the discovery/HTTP server.

nodes.name: SearchHit
nodes.summary: Search result describing a matching session with snippet parts and metadata.

nodes.name: search_sessions
nodes.summary: Full-text searches pi session files, building ranked hits with highlighted snippet parts.

nodes.name: copy_session_into_tab
nodes.summary: Copies a session file into a target tab's session directory after validating the tab id.

nodes.name: build_snippet_parts
nodes.summary: Splits matched session text into highlighted/plain snippet parts around the query.

nodes.name: export_chat_markdown
nodes.summary: Exports a session's chat transcript to formatted markdown.

nodes.name: WorkspaceStartupState
nodes.summary: Managed state tracking per-workspace startup status, tasks, and approval fingerprints.

nodes.name: parse_startup_config
nodes.summary: Parses a workspace's startup config (devshell + commands) from TOML.

nodes.name: prepare_workspace_startup_inner
nodes.summary: Core startup orchestrator: prepares the devshell env then runs configured startup commands with streamed output.

nodes.name: prepare_devshell_env
nodes.summary: Resolves and applies the workspace devshell environment for startup commands.

nodes.name: run_startup_command
nodes.summary: Runs a single startup command, streaming stdout/stderr back to the frontend.

nodes.name: workspace_startup_continue
nodes.summary: Resumes a paused/awaiting startup sequence after user approval.

nodes.name: ensure_workspace_startup_ready
nodes.summary: Ensures a workspace's startup has completed (or kicks it off) before use.

nodes.name: SubagentFile
nodes.summary: Describes a subagent definition file (name, scope, contents).

nodes.name: list_in_dir
nodes.summary: Lists subagent definition files in a directory with name sanitization.

nodes.name: scope_agents_dir
nodes.summary: Resolves the agents directory for a given scope (project vs global) with containment checks.

nodes.name: subagents_write
nodes.summary: IPC command to write a subagent definition file after safety validation.

nodes.name: UpdateInfo
nodes.summary: Serializable update availability payload (version, channel, notes) sent to the frontend.

nodes.name: discover_nightly_endpoints
nodes.summary: Queries the GitHub API to find the freshest nightly updater endpoints, falling back through prior tags.

nodes.name: check_for_updates_with_channel
nodes.summary: Checks for an available update on the selected channel and stashes the pending Update.

nodes.name: install_pending_update
nodes.summary: Backs up the current app then installs the stashed pending update.

nodes.name: nightly_candidate_urls_from_json
nodes.summary: Extracts candidate nightly asset URLs from a GitHub release JSON response.

nodes.name: mdns.rs
nodes.summary: mDNS advertiser and browser for the networked discovery daemon; advertises `_aethon._tcp.local.` with the HTTP port in TXT and browses peers, emitting host-discovered/host-removed Tauri events.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps two mdns_sd::ServiceDaemon instances (advertise + browse) with a flush helper.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Discovery server lifecycle: manages the ServerHandle/ServerState, gates the mDNS advertiser on `[server] enabled`, and starts/stops the axum HTTP server and mDNS daemons during Tauri setup.

nodes.name: io.rs
nodes.summary: Shell-tab IPC I/O commands: forwards keystrokes (`shell_input`), resizes the PTY (`shell_resize`), probes for a foreground job (`shell_is_busy`), and tears down a tab (`shell_close`).
nodes.languageNotes: Platform-gated `has_foreground_job` variants (unix tcgetpgrp vs fallback).

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module for the shell-tab lifecycle, wiring together io, open, reader, registry, and sharing submodules.

nodes.name: open.rs
nodes.summary: Spawns interactive portable-pty shell tabs, applying the resolved Nix devshell environment after TERM/COLORTERM and before per-tab overrides, and resolves the shell binary, args, and label.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest shell module; devshell env applied before args.env so explicit per-tab env wins.

nodes.name: reader.rs
nodes.summary: PTY reader thread that streams shell output as Tauri events, preserving UTF-8 boundaries across reads via a carry buffer and parsing OSC title sequences.
nodes.languageNotes: utf8_safe_split keeps multi-byte sequences intact across chunk boundaries; do not use per-chunk from_utf8_lossy.

nodes.name: registry.rs
nodes.summary: Per-tab ShellRegistry holding each shell slot's PTY writer, cwd, command, share handle, and scrollback buffer.

nodes.name: sharing.rs
nodes.summary: Opt-in shell-sharing surface: sets a tab's ShareMode, reads scrollback snapshots, and writes keystrokes into a shared shell gated by the share-mode security floor.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Top-level shell module barrel exposing the lifecycle, scrollback, and sharemode submodules.

nodes.name: scrollback.rs
nodes.summary: Bounded ring-buffer scrollback store for a shell tab, tracking oldest/total appended offsets and supporting reads from an arbitrary offset.

nodes.name: advertise
nodes.summary: Registers the `_aethon._tcp.local.` mDNS service advertising the bound HTTP port in TXT.

nodes.name: start_browser
nodes.summary: Browses for peer Aethon services and emits host-discovered/host-removed Tauri events as peers appear and leave.

nodes.name: DiscoveredHost
nodes.summary: Serializable record of a discovered peer host (name, address, port, TXT fields).

nodes.name: ServerState
nodes.summary: Managed state wrapping the running ServerHandle with start/stop/port accessors.

nodes.name: ServerHandle
nodes.summary: Handle to a running discovery server bundling the axum task and mDNS daemons for teardown.

nodes.name: boot
nodes.summary: Starts the discovery server during Tauri setup, conditionally advertising based on `[server] enabled`.

nodes.name: start
nodes.summary: Binds the axum HTTP server on an OS-assigned port and launches the mDNS advertiser and browser daemons.

nodes.name: server_advertise_enabled
nodes.summary: Reads the `[server] enabled` config flag to decide whether the mDNS advertiser runs.

nodes.name: shell_input
nodes.summary: IPC command forwarding keystrokes from the frontend into a shell tab's PTY writer.

nodes.name: shell_resize
nodes.summary: IPC command resizing a shell tab's PTY to the given rows/cols.

nodes.name: shell_is_busy
nodes.summary: Reports whether a shell tab has a foreground job running (used to guard close prompts).

nodes.name: shell_close
nodes.summary: Tears down a shell tab, killing its PTY and removing it from the registry.

nodes.name: shell_open
nodes.summary: Spawns a new interactive PTY shell tab, applying the resolved Nix devshell environment and registering the slot, reader, and scrollback.

nodes.name: devshell_launch_command_with_resolver
nodes.summary: Builds the shell launch command and environment from a devshell resolver, layering devshell env before explicit per-tab overrides.

nodes.name: devshell_effective_config
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective devshell config for a project root, honoring per-project overrides.

nodes.name: is_known_interactive_shell
nodes.summary: Classifies a shell binary path as a known interactive shell to pick login/interactive args.

nodes.name: ShellOpenArgs
nodes.summary: Wire arguments for opening a shell tab (cwd, command, env, share mode, tab id).

nodes.name: spawn_reader_thread
nodes.summary: Spawns the PTY reader thread that streams shell-output events, parses OSC titles, and emits shell-exit on EOF.

nodes.name: parse_osc_title
nodes.summary: Parses an OSC 0/2 terminal title escape sequence out of a shell output chunk.

nodes.name: utf8_safe_split
nodes.summary: Splits a byte buffer at a UTF-8 boundary, returning the carry bytes so multi-byte sequences survive across reads.

nodes.name: ShellRegistry
nodes.summary: Per-tab registry of live shell slots keyed by tab id, owning each slot's PTY writer and buffers.

nodes.name: shell_set_share_mode
nodes.summary: Sets a shell tab's ShareMode, the per-tab security boundary for opt-in sharing.

nodes.name: shell_read_scrollback
nodes.summary: Returns a scrollback snapshot for a shareable shell tab if its share mode permits reading.

nodes.name: shell_write
nodes.summary: Writes keystrokes into a shared shell, enforcing read-write share mode and the Allow/Deny prompt.

nodes.name: shell_list_shareable
nodes.summary: Lists shell tabs currently exposed for sharing, the opt-in floor enforced before any read/write.

nodes.name: Scrollback
nodes.summary: Bounded scrollback ring buffer tracking appended/oldest byte offsets for offset-based reads.

nodes.name: read_from
nodes.summary: Reads buffered scrollback bytes from a given offset, clamping to the oldest retained data.

nodes.name: registry.rs
nodes.summary: VoiceProviderRegistry: central dispatcher that lists/selects/enables providers, prepares model downloads, and routes start/stop/cancel recording, transcription, and speech synthesis to the selected provider.

nodes.name: settings.rs
nodes.summary: Persistent key/value voice settings store (selected provider, enabled flags) with get/set/delete and locked persistence.

nodes.name: types.rs
nodes.summary: Shared voice domain types and the VoiceTranscriber / VoiceProvider traits: provider kind/status/metadata/info, recording mode, level and download-progress payloads, and error events.

nodes.name: migration.rs
nodes.summary: Migrates persisted window-state files from v0 (physical pixel units) to v1 (logical units).

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Window-state module barrel exposing the WindowStateStore (generation-tracked) and the migration, monitor-matching, persistence, restore, save, and schema submodules.

nodes.name: monitor_matching.rs
nodes.summary: Three-tier monitor matching (exact -> intersects -> nearest-by-center) used to place the restored window on the right display.

nodes.name: persistence.rs
nodes.summary: Reads and writes the window-state JSON store at `~/.aethon/window-state.json`, resolving the state file path.

nodes.name: restore.rs
nodes.summary: Restores window position/size/maximized from persisted state before the window becomes visible, clamping for titlebar reachability and skipping transient fullscreen.

nodes.name: save.rs
nodes.summary: Debounced window-state save (250 ms on move/resize) plus synchronous flush, writing logical geometry and matched monitor snapshot to disk.

nodes.name: schema.rs
nodes.summary: Window-state data schema: WindowState, MonitorSnapshot, PersistedStore, and LogicalRect with monitor-from/center helpers and a sane default.

nodes.name: VoiceProviderRegistry
nodes.summary: Central voice dispatcher: lists/selects/enables providers, prepares downloads, and routes recording, transcription, and speech to the active provider.

nodes.name: start_recording
nodes.summary: Begins recording on the selected provider, dispatching to the platform, Distil, or LFM2 path.

nodes.name: stop_and_transcribe
nodes.summary: Stops the active recording and returns the transcript from the selected provider.

nodes.name: prepare_provider
nodes.summary: Triggers model download/preparation for a provider, reporting progress.

nodes.name: synthesize_speech
nodes.summary: Synthesizes speech for text via the active TTS-capable provider and plays it back.

nodes.name: resolve_provider_id
nodes.summary: Resolves an effective provider id, applying the auto/selected fallback chain.

nodes.name: stop_platform_recording
nodes.summary: Stops a platform-speech recording and returns the native recognizer transcript.

nodes.name: stop_distil_recording
nodes.summary: Stops a Distil-Whisper recording and runs candle transcription over the captured audio.

nodes.name: stop_lfm2_recording
nodes.summary: Stops an LFM2 recording and runs CLI ASR over the captured audio.

nodes.name: start_platform_recording
nodes.summary: Starts recording through the native OS speech recognizer provider.

nodes.name: start_distil_recording
nodes.summary: Starts cpal capture for a Distil-Whisper transcription run.

nodes.name: start_lfm2_recording
nodes.summary: Starts cpal capture for an LFM2-Audio ASR run.

nodes.name: remove_provider_model
nodes.summary: Deletes a provider's downloaded model files from the cache.

nodes.name: VoiceSettings
nodes.summary: Persistent voice settings store with get/set/delete and locked write-back to disk.

nodes.name: VoiceProvider
nodes.summary: Trait every voice provider implements: id, metadata, status, and model preparation.

nodes.name: VoiceTranscriber
nodes.summary: Trait for transcribing captured audio into text.

nodes.name: VoiceProviderMetadata
nodes.summary: Static provider metadata (id, name, model, accelerator, privacy labels) surfaced to the UI.

nodes.name: VoiceProviderInfo
nodes.summary: Runtime provider info combining metadata with live status and enabled state.

nodes.name: migrate
nodes.summary: Migrates a persisted window-state store from v0 physical-pixel units to v1 logical units.

nodes.name: WindowStateStore
nodes.summary: Managed window-state store tracking a save generation counter for debounced persistence.

nodes.name: intersects
nodes.summary: Finds a monitor whose bounds intersect the saved window rect (tier two of monitor matching).

nodes.name: nearest_by_center
nodes.summary: Finds the monitor nearest the saved window center (fallback tier of monitor matching).

nodes.name: exact_match
nodes.summary: Finds a monitor exactly matching the saved monitor snapshot (tier one of monitor matching).

nodes.name: load_store
nodes.summary: Loads the persisted window-state store JSON, running migration on older versions.

nodes.name: save_store
nodes.summary: Serializes the window-state store to `~/.aethon/window-state.json`.

nodes.name: restore_window
nodes.summary: Applies persisted position/size/maximized to the window, matching the monitor and clamping for titlebar reachability.

nodes.name: restoreable_state
nodes.summary: Computes the restoreable window geometry from saved state and the current monitor layout.

nodes.name: show_window
nodes.summary: Shows the previously-hidden window after geometry restore completes.

nodes.name: schedule_save
nodes.summary: Debounces a window-state save 250 ms after a move/resize, bumping the generation counter.

nodes.name: save_now
nodes.summary: Captures the current window geometry and monitor snapshot and writes it to disk synchronously.

nodes.name: WindowState
nodes.summary: Persisted window geometry record (logical rect, maximized flag, monitor snapshot).

nodes.name: MonitorSnapshot
nodes.summary: Snapshot of a monitor (name, logical bounds, scale) used to re-match the display on restore.

nodes.name: from_monitor
nodes.summary: Builds a MonitorSnapshot from a live Tauri monitor handle in logical units.

nodes.name: default_normal
nodes.summary: Produces a sensible default normal-window geometry when no saved state exists.

nodes.name: sharemode.rs
nodes.summary: ShareMode enum and ShareState transition logic that enforces the per-tab shell-sharing security boundary (none / read-only / read-write / read-write-trusted).

nodes.name: updater_state.rs
nodes.summary: Managed UpdaterState holding the non-serializable pending Update between a check and install, plus boot-probation wiring.

nodes.name: audio.rs
nodes.summary: cpal-based audio recorder: captures PCM into a RecordingSession, meters levels via a spawned emitter task, and normalizes/resamples interleaved samples of every sample format to mono target-rate f32.
nodes.languageNotes: Exhaustive per-sample-format normalize_interleaved_* helpers cover cpal's full SampleFormat matrix.

nodes.name: download.rs
nodes.summary: Downloads voice model weights (Distil-Whisper, LFM2) on demand from Hugging Face via a shared download_hf_model helper.

nodes.name: inference.rs
nodes.summary: Candle-backed Whisper transcription: selects the candle device/backend, verifies the backend with a probe op, and runs the encoder/decoder decode loop including language detection and greedy token sampling.
nodes.languageNotes: Backend selection is platform-gated (Metal/CUDA/CPU); verify_candle_whisper_backend probes a real op to fail fast.

nodes.name: lfm2.rs
nodes.summary: LFM2-Audio CLI backend for ASR and TTS: resolves the bundled/installed llama.cpp runner binary, writes input WAV, runs the CLI capturing stdout, and parses ASR transcript / diagnostic lines.

nodes.name: mel.rs
nodes.summary: Mel filterbank construction and hz<->mel conversion helpers used by the Whisper feature extractor.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Voice subsystem core: holds the active provider engine and orchestrates recording, transcription, and playback across Whisper, platform speech, and LFM2 providers with no per-OS branching at this layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest file in the batch (~2157 lines); holds a &dyn PlatformSpeechEngine so providers share one CapturedAudio buffer.

nodes.name: playback.rs
nodes.summary: cpal audio playback engine: feeds a PlaybackBuffer through a typed output stream, tracks play state, and spawns a watcher that fires on completion.

nodes.name: providers.rs
nodes.summary: Voice provider implementations (platform speech, Distil-Whisper candle, LFM2) with platform-specific recording mode / privacy / accelerator labels and model-readiness checks behind a common provider trait.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of cfg-gated platform_* fns to surface per-OS metadata through one trait.

nodes.name: ShareMode
nodes.summary: Enum of shell share modes (none/read-only/read-write/read-write-trusted) with shareable/write predicates.

nodes.name: ShareState
nodes.summary: Per-tab share state with guarded transition logic enforcing the sharing security boundary.

nodes.name: UpdaterState
nodes.summary: Managed state holding the non-serializable pending Update between an update check and install.

nodes.name: start
nodes.summary: Opens the default cpal input device, captures interleaved PCM into a RecordingSession, and spawns the level-metering emitter.

nodes.name: spawn_level_emitter
nodes.summary: Spawns a task that computes RMS over the captured buffer and emits voice-level meter events.

nodes.name: mix_interleaved_to_mono
nodes.summary: Mixes an interleaved multi-channel f32 buffer down to a single mono channel.

nodes.name: resample
nodes.summary: Linearly resamples a mono f32 buffer from a source to target sample rate.

nodes.name: RecordingSession
nodes.summary: Active recording session holding the cpal stream, captured samples, and stream-error channel.

nodes.name: download_hf_model
nodes.summary: Downloads a set of model files from a Hugging Face repo into the local cache with progress reporting.

nodes.name: verify_candle_whisper_backend
nodes.summary: Probes a real candle op to verify the selected Whisper backend works, failing fast on a broken accelerator.

nodes.name: transcribe_distil_whisper
nodes.summary: Runs full Distil-Whisper transcription over captured audio, returning the decoded transcript.

nodes.name: decoder_prompt_tokens
nodes.summary: Builds the Whisper decoder prompt token sequence (task, language, timestamps) for a decode run.

nodes.name: WhisperDecoder
nodes.summary: Whisper decode loop: language detection, greedy token sampling, and final transcript assembly.

nodes.name: WhisperModel
nodes.summary: Loaded Whisper model wrapper exposing encoder/decoder forward passes and final linear projection.

nodes.name: CandleBackend
nodes.summary: Selected candle compute backend (device + accelerator label) for Whisper inference.

nodes.name: decode
nodes.summary: Runs one Whisper decoder pass producing tokens and per-token probabilities.

nodes.name: detect_language_token
nodes.summary: Detects the spoken-language token from the Whisper decoder's first-step logits.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Drives the Whisper decoder across audio segments to assemble the full transcription result.

nodes.name: asr
nodes.summary: Runs LFM2-Audio ASR by writing an input WAV and invoking the CLI runner, parsing the transcript from stdout.

nodes.name: tts
nodes.summary: Runs LFM2-Audio TTS via the CLI runner and reads back the synthesized output WAV.

nodes.name: run_cli_capture
nodes.summary: Spawns the LFM2 CLI runner subprocess, capturing stdout/stderr and the exit status.

nodes.name: read_output_wav
nodes.summary: Reads and decodes the WAV file produced by the LFM2 TTS CLI into PCM samples.

nodes.name: Lfm2CliBackend
nodes.summary: LFM2 CLI backend wrapping the resolved runner binary and model cache path for ASR/TTS.

nodes.name: bundled_lfm2_binary
nodes.summary: Resolves the bundled LFM2 runner binary path inside the app resources.

nodes.name: build_mel_filters
nodes.summary: Builds the mel filterbank matrix used to convert spectrogram bins to mel features.

nodes.name: play_samples
nodes.summary: Plays a PCM sample buffer through a cpal output stream and tracks play state.

nodes.name: build_output_stream
nodes.summary: Builds a cpal output stream that drains the PlaybackBuffer one sample at a time.

nodes.name: typed_output_stream
nodes.summary: Constructs a sample-format-typed cpal output stream for playback.

nodes.name: spawn_playback_watcher
nodes.summary: Spawns a watcher that fires a completion callback when playback drains.

nodes.name: AudioPlayer
nodes.summary: cpal-backed audio player owning the output stream and current playback buffer.

nodes.name: PlaybackBuffer
nodes.summary: Sample buffer feeding the playback stream, tracking the next-sample cursor and finished state.

nodes.name: platform_status_from_availability
nodes.summary: Maps platform speech availability into a provider status (ready / needs-permission / unavailable).

nodes.name: model_files_ready
nodes.summary: Checks whether a provider's required model files are present in the cache.

nodes.name: PlatformVoiceProvider
nodes.summary: Provider backed by the native OS speech recognizer, surfacing per-OS metadata and status.

nodes.name: DistilWhisperCandleProvider
nodes.summary: Provider running Distil-Whisper locally via candle, with model-download preparation.

nodes.name: Lfm2AudioProvider
nodes.summary: Provider running LFM2-Audio ASR/TTS via the CLI runner, with model-readiness checks.

nodes.name: useBridgeMessages.ts
nodes.summary: Core hook that wires the agent-response Tauri event into a batched payload pump, dispatches each message to its registered handler, and origin-gates registry-mutating messages to the active workspace.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements an input-pending-aware cooperative scheduler (createBridgePayloadPump) to avoid blocking the UI thread.

nodes.name: useControlRequests.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for useControlRequests covering tab open/close/focus, account switching, chat dispatch, and the idle/timeout race logic.

nodes.name: useControlRequests.ts
nodes.summary: Hook implementing the agent-control protocol: listens for control requests and executes tab lifecycle, account, chat, wait-for-idle, and stop-agent operations, completing each request back to the bridge.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a busy->idle polling race plus a deterministic control-wait registry to detect turn completion.

nodes.name: nativeWindows.ts
nodes.summary: Pure helpers for native canvas/terminal windows: normalizing window records and canvas components, summarizing them, and syncing the native-window set into app state.

nodes.name: voice.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the voice service wrapper verifying each Tauri voice command (provider list/select, prepare/remove, record/transcribe, speak/stop) is invoked correctly.

nodes.name: tauriMocks.test.ts
nodes.summary: Self-test for the Tauri mock harness verifying invoke/listen/emit stubbing and event fan-out.

nodes.name: tauriMocks.ts
nodes.summary: Test harness that installs vitest mocks for the Tauri invoke/listen/emit APIs and provides a fireEvent helper to drive registered listeners.

nodes.name: agentBusy.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the agent-busy predicates covering running-tool-card detection and in-flight tab classification.

nodes.name: agentBusy.ts
nodes.summary: Utility predicates and transforms for agent activity: detecting running tool cards, classifying a tab as busy/in-flight, and force-closing running tool cards on stop/crash.

nodes.name: highlight.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the syntax-highlight utility covering LRU caching, worker messaging, and grammar registration.

nodes.name: highlight.ts
nodes.summary: Syntax-highlighting utility backed by a web worker with an LRU result cache, async highlight requests, prewarming, and dynamic grammar registration.
nodes.languageNotes: Offloads highlighting to a Worker and maintains a bounded LRU keyed by lang+code hash.

nodes.name: messages.ts
nodes.summary: Chat-message utilities: extracting last agent text, trimming/slimming A2UI payloads for durable storage, deduping tool-result text, collapsing amended agent messages, and coercing persisted chat history.
nodes.languageNotes: Enforces byte budgets on A2UI payloads and strips inline image data URLs before persistence.

nodes.name: stateMutation.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the state-mutation helpers covering JSON-pointer layout patching and deep state merging.

nodes.name: stateMutation.ts
nodes.summary: Pure helpers for mutating layout payloads via JSON pointer and deep-merging plain-object state trees.

nodes.name: toolCardIdentity.ts
nodes.summary: Small utility that normalizes tool-call ids and derives a stable tool-card identity (entry id and tool-call id) from a component id.

nodes.name: useBridgeMessages
nodes.summary: React hook that boots the agent, listens for agent-response events, pumps payloads cooperatively, and dispatches each to its handler with mutation acking.

nodes.name: createBridgePayloadPump
nodes.summary: Builds an input-pending-aware payload pump that drains queued bridge payloads in time-sliced batches to keep the UI responsive.

nodes.name: bridgeDispatchDecision
nodes.summary: Decides whether a bridge message should be dispatched, ack-rejected, or dropped based on origin-tab gating against the active workspace.

nodes.name: useControlRequests
nodes.summary: Hook that subscribes to agent-control requests and dispatches each to its handler, completing the request back to the bridge.

nodes.name: sendChat
nodes.summary: Control operation that resolves the target tab/account, sends a chat message, and registers a control-wait for turn completion.

nodes.name: raceControlWait
nodes.summary: Races a busy->idle polling loop against the deterministic control-wait registry to detect whether a control-sent turn completed, idled, or timed out.

nodes.name: handleControlRequest
nodes.summary: Routes a control request to the appropriate operation (open/close/focus tab, account, chat, wait-for-idle, stop).

nodes.name: waitForIdle
nodes.summary: Control operation that polls a tab until it transitions to idle or the timeout elapses.

nodes.name: normalizeCanvasComponents
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw canvas component input into a validated array of canvas components, dropping invalid entries.

nodes.name: summarizeNativeWindow
nodes.summary: Reduces a native window record to a serializable summary for app state.

nodes.name: syncNativeWindowsToState
nodes.summary: Sorts and writes the current native-window summaries into app state via setState.

nodes.name: terminalShellTabIds
nodes.summary: Extracts the owned terminal shell tab ids from a native window record.

nodes.name: installTauriMocks
nodes.summary: Installs vitest mocks for Tauri invoke/listen/emit and wires a per-event handler registry returning a controllable harness.

nodes.name: clearTauriMocks
nodes.summary: Restores all vitest mocks and resets the Tauri invoke/listen/emit stubs to no-op resolutions.

nodes.name: closeRunningToolCards
nodes.summary: Walks chat messages and force-closes any running tool cards (recursively through components), stamping an ended status and notice.

nodes.name: closeRunningToolCardComponent
nodes.summary: Recursively closes a running tool-card component, appending a closed notice and marking children done.

nodes.name: isAgentTabBusy
nodes.summary: Determines whether an agent tab is busy, accounting for in-flight prompts and running tool cards.

nodes.name: highlightCode
nodes.summary: Asynchronously highlights code via the worker, returning a cached result when available and registering a pending promise otherwise.

nodes.name: getWorker
nodes.summary: Lazily creates the highlight web worker, wiring its message/error handlers to resolve pending requests.

nodes.name: registerGrammar
nodes.summary: Registers a dynamic highlight grammar with the worker and invalidates cached results for that language.

nodes.name: coerceChatMessages
nodes.summary: Coerces an untrusted persisted value into a validated array of chat messages, normalizing attachments, ids, and timestamps then collapsing amended messages.

nodes.name: stripImageDataUrls
nodes.summary: Recursively strips inline image data URLs from an A2UI component tree to keep persisted payloads small.

nodes.name: trimMessage
nodes.summary: Trims a chat message to fit byte budgets, slimming its text and A2UI payload for durable storage.

nodes.name: collapseAmendedAgentMessages
nodes.summary: Collapses sequences of amended agent messages sharing an id down to their latest non-empty form.

nodes.name: dedupeToolResultTextMessages
nodes.summary: Removes redundant tool-result text messages that duplicate a tool's already-rendered output.

nodes.name: lastAgentText
nodes.summary: Returns the trimmed text of the most recent non-empty agent message.

nodes.name: layoutPatch
nodes.summary: Applies a value at a JSON-pointer path within a layout payload, cloning nodes along the way to keep the patch immutable.

nodes.name: deepMergeState
nodes.summary: Recursively deep-merges plain-object source into target, replacing non-object values.

nodes.name: toolCardIdentityFromId
nodes.summary: Derives a stable tool-card identity (entry id plus normalized tool-call id) from a component id.

nodes.name: statePatch.ts

nodes.name: agentStderr.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the agent-stderr OS-edge subscriber verifying severity filtering and chat-message synthesis from stderr lines.

nodes.summary: OS-edge subscriber that listens to agent stderr, filters by severity, and surfaces WARN/ERROR/FATAL lines as diagnostic chat messages on the owning tab.

nodes.name: handleStatePatch

nodes.name: applyProgressPhase
nodes.summary: Folds a single progress event (delta text, tool step, or error) into the current subagent progress card, capping accumulated text and step history.

nodes.name: handleTabReady

nodes.summary: Drains the pending terminal buffer for a tab and mirrors its content into state.

nodes.name: buildHandlerFixture
nodes.summary: Constructs a fully-stubbed BridgeMessageContext with vi.fn mocks, refs, registry, and project state for use across bridge-handler tests.

nodes.name: registerControlWait
nodes.summary: Registers a pending control-wait promise for a request id with a timeout that resolves to a timeout outcome.

nodes.name: resolveControlWait
nodes.summary: Resolves a registered control-wait with a given outcome, clearing its timer and removing it from the registry.

nodes.name: cancelControlWait
nodes.summary: Cancels a pending control-wait, clearing its timer and resolving it as cancelled.

nodes.name: subscribeAgentStderr
nodes.summary: Subscribes to the agent stderr Tauri event, filters lines by severity, and appends/persists diagnostic chat messages for the owning tab.

nodes.name: useAppBridgeMessages
nodes.summary: App-level adapter hook that forwards boot layout and error callbacks into useBridgeMessages.

nodes.name: terminal.ts
nodes.summary: Emits the buffered agent-bash terminal result for a tab to the read-only terminal panel stream.

nodes.name: emitBashResult
nodes.summary: Formats and sends a completed bash tool result to the agent-bash terminal stream for a tab.

nodes.name: tools.ts
nodes.summary: Cancels any in-flight tool cards for a tab, emitting cancelled tool-card payloads so the UI clears running indicators.

nodes.name: cancelRunningToolCards
nodes.summary: Iterates a tab's running tool cards, marks them cancelled, and emits the updated payloads to the frontend.

nodes.name: utils.ts

nodes.name: modelDescriptor
nodes.summary: Derives a structured model descriptor (provider/id/thinking) from a model record for the picker and prompts.

nodes.name: modelKey
nodes.summary: Computes the canonical key string for a model used to dedupe and address picker entries.

nodes.name: tabs.ts
nodes.summary: Handles tab-level bridge messages: opening a tab (parsing model+thinking, attaching session/extensions), closing a tab, setting its session label, and persisting local chat messages.
nodes.languageNotes: Each handler is a bridge message entry point; handleTabOpen wires session history, auth profiles, and extension loading for a newly opened tab.

nodes.name: handleTabOpen
nodes.summary: Opens a tab: parses its model and thinking level, ensures its session, attaches session history and auth/extension context, and emits the ready handshake.

nodes.name: parseModelAndThinking
nodes.summary: Parses a combined model+thinking-level descriptor string into its model id and normalized thinking level.

nodes.name: handleTabClose
nodes.summary: Closes a tab, disposing its session and cleaning up associated lifecycle state.

nodes.name: handleSetSessionLabel
nodes.summary: Sets and persists a custom session label for a tab and emits the updated session summary.

nodes.name: handleLocalChatMessage
nodes.summary: Persists a locally composed chat message into the tab's session history (including attachments) without invoking the model.

nodes.name: terminal-stream.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the bash terminal snapshot consumer, verifying overlap-aware incremental output extraction.

nodes.name: terminal-stream.ts
nodes.summary: Computes the incremental delta between successive bash terminal snapshots using suffix/prefix overlap (KMP-style prefix table) so the agent-bash stream only emits newly appended output.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a KMP-style longest-suffix-prefix overlap to dedupe overlapping snapshot text instead of naive diffing.

nodes.name: consumeBashTerminalSnapshot
nodes.summary: Returns the newly appended portion of a bash terminal snapshot relative to the previously consumed buffer using overlap detection.

nodes.name: longestSuffixPrefixOverlap
nodes.summary: Finds the longest overlap where the suffix of one string equals the prefix of another, used to align consecutive terminal snapshots.

nodes.name: buildPrefixTable
nodes.summary: Builds the KMP prefix-function table used by the overlap computation.

nodes.name: tool-card.ts
nodes.summary: Builds the structured tool-card payloads shown in chat: summarizes tool arguments, infers result language, extracts and truncates tool output, and computes file-change/diff stats for display.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-tool argument summarization and language inference drive how each tool invocation renders as a card in the chat transcript.

nodes.name: summarizeToolArgs
nodes.summary: Produces a compact human-readable summary of a tool call's arguments tailored per tool type.

nodes.name: fileChangeForTool
nodes.summary: Derives the file-change descriptor (path, created/modified, diff stats) for editor/write tool invocations.

nodes.name: inferToolResultLanguage
nodes.summary: Infers a syntax-highlighting language for a tool result from the tool type and target path.

nodes.name: extractToolContent
nodes.summary: Extracts and normalizes the displayable text content from a tool result's block array.

nodes.name: toolCardPayload
nodes.summary: Assembles the full tool-card payload for the chat UI from a tool call and its result, combining the argument summary, content, language, and file-change info.

nodes.name: window-tools.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the window tools, stubbing the windows API and asserting each tool's execute behavior and error handling.

nodes.name: window-tools.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the agent's window-control tools (list/inspect/manipulate app windows) backed by the windows runtime API, with consistent failure and JSON-result handling.

nodes.name: buildWindowTools
nodes.summary: Builds the set of window-control tool definitions exposed to the agent, wiring each to the windows runtime API.

nodes.name: getWindowsApi
nodes.summary: Resolves the windows runtime API from globalThis, returning a typed accessor used by the window tools.

nodes.name: working-context.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the working-context section builder, asserting rendered git/cwd/anchor lines for various inputs.

nodes.name: working-context.ts
nodes.summary: Builds the working-context markdown section of the system prompt from the current cwd, git context, and an optional soft anchor.

nodes.name: buildWorkingContextSection

nodes.name: tab-lifecycle.test.ts
nodes.summary: Large integration test suite for the tab-lifecycle module, exercising session-event handling, model picker refresh, retry scheduling, ready handshake, and tool-card emission via fixtures.

nodes.name: fakeRec
nodes.summary: Test fixture that fabricates a tab record with a parsed model string for driving tab-lifecycle assertions.

nodes.name: events.ts
nodes.summary: Core session-event handler for a tab: streams assistant text/thinking/tool events into session messages and IDs, manages response segmentation, compaction notices, context-overflow recovery, and final-content reconciliation.
nodes.languageNotes: handleSessionEvent is a ~465-line dispatcher fanning each pi event kind out to specialized emit helpers; context-overflow recovery races a compaction fallback timer.

nodes.name: handleSessionEvent
nodes.summary: Central dispatcher that processes each streamed pi session event for a tab, updating response state, emitting session/tool events, and triggering compaction and recovery flows.

nodes.name: emitFinalAssistantContent
nodes.summary: Reconciles streamed deltas against the final assistant message and emits any missing suffix so the persisted content matches the model output.

nodes.name: startContextOverflowRecovery
nodes.summary: Begins the context-overflow recovery flow for a tab when the model reports the context length was exceeded.

nodes.name: runContextOverflowRecoveryFallback
nodes.summary: Fallback path that compacts the session and retries when automatic context-overflow recovery stalls.

nodes.name: continueAfterContextCompaction
nodes.summary: Resumes the interrupted turn after a context compaction completes, re-prompting the session.

nodes.name: handleContextOverflowCompactionEvent
nodes.summary: Handles compaction lifecycle events during context-overflow recovery, advancing the recovery state machine.

nodes.name: emitResponseSessionEvent
nodes.summary: Emits a session event for a response message segment with stable message and previous-message IDs.

nodes.name: finalizeFailedTurn
nodes.summary: Finalizes a turn that ended in failure, recording the trailing failure message for retry handling.

nodes.name: emitEntryIds
nodes.summary: Emits the canonical entry IDs for streamed/session messages so the frontend can address them stably.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports the tab-lifecycle submodules (events, models, lifecycle, retry, slash-commands, ready-handshake, utils, tools, terminal) as a single public surface.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript barrel aggregating 27 re-exports; no logic of its own.

nodes.name: lifecycle.ts
nodes.summary: Owns tab creation/initialization: resolves a tab's cwd and lazily builds its pi session with the full agent toolset (bash, editor, dashboard, memory, shell, scheduler, session, window, subagent task tools) plus auth, devshell, and codex-fast-mode wiring.
nodes.languageNotes: ensureTab assembles the tab's entire tool registry from sibling builder modules; cwd resolution precedence is override > recorded > active project.

nodes.name: resolveTabCwd
nodes.summary: Resolves the working directory for a tab using the override > recorded > active-project precedence.

nodes.name: ensureTab
nodes.summary: Ensures a tab has a live pi session, lazily creating it with the assembled tool registry, auth services, devshell hook, and event subscription on first use.

nodes.name: models.ts
nodes.summary: Builds and refreshes the model picker for a tab: dedupes available models across registries, computes the default model key, and keeps the cached picker in sync with the tab's current model.

nodes.name: buildPickerModels
nodes.summary: Builds the deduplicated model picker entry list for a tab from all available registry models.

nodes.name: refreshCachedModels
nodes.summary: Recomputes and caches the tab's model picker, reconciling the current selection against the refreshed registry.

nodes.name: ensurePickerHasModel
nodes.summary: Ensures a given model id is present in the tab's picker, inserting it when missing so the active model is always selectable.

nodes.name: defaultModelKey
nodes.summary: Returns the default model key used when a tab has no explicit model selection.

nodes.name: ready-handshake.ts
nodes.summary: Emits the per-tab ready handshake to the frontend once a tab's session is initialized, bundling model picker, slash commands, auth profiles, codex-fast-mode support, and context usage into the ready payload.

nodes.name: emitReady
nodes.summary: Assembles and sends the tab ready payload (models, slash commands, auth, fast-mode, context usage) to the frontend.

nodes.name: retry.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the Aethon retry classifier and scheduler: installs a retryability classifier on the session and schedules/cancels automatic retries of failed turns, removing the trailing failure message before re-prompting.

nodes.name: scheduleAethonRetry
nodes.summary: Schedules an automatic retry of a failed turn with backoff, removing the trailing failure message and re-prompting the session.

nodes.name: installAethonRetryClassifier
nodes.summary: Installs the retryability classifier on a session so transient agent-end errors are eligible for automatic retry.

nodes.name: removeTrailingFailureMessage
nodes.summary: Removes a previously recorded trailing failure message from the session before a retry so it doesn't pollute the transcript.

nodes.name: cancelAethonRetry
nodes.summary: Cancels any pending scheduled retry for a tab.

nodes.name: slash-commands.ts
nodes.summary: Collects the pi session's available slash commands for a tab and refreshes the frontend picker only when the set changes.

nodes.name: collectPiSlashCommands
nodes.summary: Gathers the available pi slash commands for a tab's session into the frontend-facing command list.

nodes.name: refreshPiSlashCommands
nodes.summary: Refreshes and emits the tab's slash-command list to the frontend when it differs from the cached set.

nodes.name: parse.ts
nodes.summary: Pure parser that turns a subagent markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) into a validated Subagent definition, sanitizing the name and normalizing tools/surface/timeout fields.
nodes.languageNotes: Frontmatter is regex-extracted then parsed with a YAML library; per-field coercion functions keep one malformed value from rejecting the whole file.

nodes.name: sanitizeSubagentName
nodes.summary: Normalizes an arbitrary file stem into a safe canonical subagent name (lowercase, hyphen-collapsed, length-clamped).

nodes.name: parseSubagentMarkdown
nodes.summary: Parses a subagent markdown definition: extracts and parses YAML frontmatter, validates the description, and assembles a Subagent with model, tools, surface, timeout, and the body as system prompt.

nodes.name: parseToolsField
nodes.summary: Coerces a frontmatter tools value (array or comma-separated string) into a normalized string array tool allowlist.

nodes.name: parseTimeoutField
nodes.summary: Coerces and clamps a frontmatter timeout value into a finite, bounded per-subagent timeout in seconds.

nodes.name: resolveSubagentTools
nodes.summary: Resolves a subagent's effective tool set from its declared allowlist, distinguishing inherit-all from an empty reasoning-only lockdown.

nodes.name: isSafeSubagentName
nodes.summary: Tests whether a string is already a safe canonical subagent name against the allowed-character regex.

nodes.name: steer.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the subagent mention/steer detection helpers, covering leading @-mentions, background-intent phrases, and explicit steer-prompt construction.

nodes.name: steer.ts

nodes.languageNotes: Mention chaining handles comma/and-separated @name lists at the start of a message.

nodes.name: detectLeadingSubagentMentions
nodes.summary: Scans the start of a message for one or more chained @name subagent mentions, returning the list of mentioned subagent names.

nodes.name: buildExplicitSubagentSteer
nodes.summary: Builds the steering instruction injected into the agent turn so it delegates to the explicitly mentioned subagent(s) via the task tool.

nodes.name: detectSubagentMention
nodes.summary: Returns whether a message contains any leading subagent @-mention.

nodes.name: detectBackgroundSubagentIntent
nodes.summary: Detects phrasing that signals the user wants a subagent to run in the background (its own tab).

nodes.name: task-tool.test.ts
nodes.summary: Extensive unit tests for the subagent task and task-batch tools, exercising inline execution, model resolution, abort/cleanup, timeouts, progress emission, and background tab launch.

nodes.name: makeStateWithSubagents

nodes.name: task-tool.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the agent's `task` and `task_batch` tools that delegate focused work to subagents, running them inline in an isolated pi session or launching a background agent tab, with model resolution, file-reference expansion, progress streaming, timeouts, and abort handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Inline runs create an in-memory pi SessionManager with a per-subagent tool allowlist and devshell-wrapped bash; abort races a cleanup timeout to avoid leaking sessions.

nodes.name: buildSubagentTaskTool
nodes.summary: Defines the single-task `task` tool whose execute path delegates a prompt to one named subagent.

nodes.name: buildSubagentTaskBatchTool
nodes.summary: Defines the `task_batch` tool that fans out multiple subagent tasks concurrently and aggregates their results.

nodes.name: runSubagentTask
nodes.summary: Resolves the requested subagent, composes its prompt (expanding file references), and dispatches it either to a background tab or an inline run.

nodes.name: runSubagentTaskBatch
nodes.summary: Runs a batch of subagent tasks via Promise.allSettled, streaming per-task partial output and assembling a combined success/failure report.

nodes.name: resolveModelServices
nodes.summary: Resolves the pi services and model registry entry for a subagent's requested model, falling back to the parent tab's auth-profile services.

nodes.name: runInlineSubagent
nodes.summary: Runs a subagent inline in an in-memory pi session: builds its tool allowlist and bash hook, subscribes to streamed events, enforces a timeout, and handles abort cleanup before returning the final assistant text.

nodes.name: waitForAbortCleanup
nodes.summary: Races an abort-cleanup promise against a bounded timeout so a wedged subagent session never blocks the delegating turn indefinitely.

nodes.name: launchSubagentTab
nodes.summary: Launches a subagent as its own background agent tab through the tasks API, stripping expanded file references from the displayed prompt.

nodes.name: handleSubagentEvent
nodes.summary: Translates a streamed pi session event from an inline subagent into the task tool's text/thinking/tool/end callbacks.

nodes.name: extractLastAssistantText

nodes.name: emitProgress
nodes.summary: Sends a structured progress update for a running subagent task back to the frontend via the deps send channel.

nodes.name: withTimeout
nodes.summary: Wraps a promise with a timeout that rejects (and invokes an onTimeout hook) if it doesn't settle within the given milliseconds.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for subagents: the Subagent definition shape, scope/surface enums, load-issue records, and the merged per-cwd registry result.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure data + JSDoc; IO and parsing live in sibling loader/parse modules.

nodes.name: system-prompt.test.ts

nodes.summary: Holds the static default Aethon system-prompt template string used as the base layer before runtime context is appended.

nodes.summary: Type definitions for the runtime snapshot contract consumed by the system-prompt builder (UI/component/theme/slot/layout shapes).
