nodes.summary: Pure parser extracting hunk kind, start, and count from unified-diff @@ headers, clamping top-of-file insertions to line one.

nodes.name: parse_numstat
nodes.summary: Pure parser summing the insertion/deletion columns of git numstat output, skipping binary entries.

nodes.name: github.rs
nodes.summary: Largest GitHub-integration module: gh-CLI commands for branch push/PR status, repository overview metadata, and avatar URL discovery, plus dangling-worktree detection that suppresses gh calls for broken worktrees.
nodes.languageNotes: Each public command delegates to a testable _inner; worktree_is_dangling/normalize_path guard against gh failures inside dangling worktrees, and url_encode_path_segment percent-encodes slash-bearing branch names before the branches API call.

nodes.name: GhBranchStatus
nodes.summary: Serde payload describing a branch's remote push state, associated PRs, and whether its worktree is broken.

nodes.name: GhPr
nodes.summary: Serde model for a pull request summary (number, state, title, URL, draft/merged flags, base ref).

nodes.name: GhRepoOverview
nodes.summary: Serde payload aggregating repository metadata (description, URL, default branch, stars, forks, open issue/PR counts, last push) for the dashboard.

nodes.name: gh_branch_status_inner
nodes.summary: Resolves the repo, checks gh auth, queries remote branch existence and open PRs for a branch, and assembles GhBranchStatus; the testable core of gh_branch_status.

nodes.name: gh_repo_overview_inner
nodes.summary: Fetches repo view metadata plus open PR and issue counts via concurrent gh calls and builds the GhRepoOverview payload.

nodes.name: gh_repo_avatar_url
nodes.summary: Tauri command that resolves the owner's avatar URL for a repository via the gh API.

nodes.name: worktree_is_dangling
nodes.summary: Detects a broken worktree whose gitdir marker no longer resolves, so gh calls can be short-circuited rather than erroring.

nodes.name: normalize_path
nodes.summary: Lexically normalizes a path (resolving . and .. without touching disk) for worktree-marker comparison.

nodes.name: parse_repo_view_json
nodes.summary: Pure parser mapping the gh repo view JSON into the RepoViewParts struct consumed by the overview builder.

nodes.name: url_encode_path_segment
nodes.summary: Percent-encodes a path segment (notably slash-bearing branch names) for safe interpolation into gh API routes.

nodes.name: issues.rs
nodes.summary: Tauri commands for listing open GitHub issues and viewing a single issue's detail via the gh CLI, with a pure JSON parser for the list response.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses gh issue list with an explicit limit (counted via -q length elsewhere) and parses labels/author/comments into typed serde models.

nodes.name: GhIssue
nodes.summary: Serde model for an issue summary row (number, title, URL, state, labels, updated time, author, comment count).

nodes.name: GhIssueDetail
nodes.summary: Serde model for a single issue's full detail (number, title, URL, body, author).

nodes.name: parse_gh_issue_list
nodes.summary: Pure parser converting gh issue list JSON into GhIssue records, extracting labels and author fields.

nodes.name: gh_issue_list
nodes.summary: Tauri command that shells out to gh issue list for open issues and returns the parsed records.

nodes.name: gh_issue_view
nodes.summary: Tauri command that fetches a single issue's detail via gh issue view and returns a GhIssueDetail.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Module barrel for the git command family, declaring and re-exporting the checks, common, diff, github, issues, picker, status, watch, and worktrees submodules.

nodes.name: picker.rs
nodes.summary: Tauri command exposing a native directory picker dialog for selecting a project root.

nodes.name: pick_project_directory
nodes.summary: Opens a native folder-selection dialog and returns the chosen project directory path.

nodes.name: status.rs
nodes.summary: Core git working-tree status module: branch/ahead/behind/dirty summary, per-file porcelain-z status classification, working context resolution, fetch-all with attempt reservation, and ignored-path discovery — all worktree-aware against the active root.
nodes.languageNotes: Parses porcelain=v1 --branch and porcelain=v2 -z NUL-delimited output; resolve_repo_and_active_root and path_relative_to_active_root make a workspace report its own branch and rebase paths into the active worktree.

nodes.name: GitStatus
nodes.summary: Serde model for the high-level status summary (branch, dirty flag, ahead/behind counts).

nodes.name: GitFileStatusEntry
nodes.summary: Serde model for one changed file's path, status code, and optional original (rename source) path.

nodes.name: GitWorkingContext
nodes.summary: Serde model summarizing a workspace's repo root, branch, worktree flag, changed-file count, and ahead/behind for git-context callers.

nodes.name: git_fetch_all_inner
nodes.summary: Runs git fetch --all for a project, coordinating with GitFetchState to throttle concurrent fetch attempts per repo.

nodes.name: git_status
nodes.summary: Primary Tauri command producing the branch/dirty/ahead/behind status summary from porcelain output, guarding against a locked index.

nodes.name: git_working_context
nodes.summary: Resolves the active root, branch, worktree flag, and change counts into a GitWorkingContext for a cwd.

nodes.name: git_file_status
nodes.summary: Tauri command producing the per-file changed-path breakdown from porcelain v2 -z output, rebased to the active root.

nodes.name: resolve_repo_and_active_root
nodes.summary: Resolves both the repository root and the active worktree root for a directory so status reporting stays worktree-scoped.

nodes.name: git_ignored_paths
nodes.summary: Tauri command enumerating gitignored paths under a root via git status --ignored porcelain output.

nodes.name: parse_branch_porcelain_counts
nodes.summary: Pure parser extracting ahead/behind counts from the porcelain --branch header line.

nodes.name: classify_git_status
nodes.summary: Maps a porcelain XY status pair to a normalized file-status classification for the frontend.

nodes.name: parse_git_file_status_porcelain_z
nodes.summary: Pure parser splitting NUL-delimited porcelain v2 output into GitFileStatusEntry records, handling rename source/destination pairs.

nodes.name: test_support.rs
nodes.summary: Test helper that initializes a throwaway git repository on disk for the git command module's unit and integration tests.

nodes.name: init_repo
nodes.summary: Initializes a git repo with a deterministic identity and initial commit at the given path for tests.

nodes.name: watch.rs
nodes.summary: Filesystem-watcher commands that monitor a repo's git internals and working tree, emitting git-state-changed events to the frontend so VCS status can refresh on real changes.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves both the per-worktree git-dir and shared git-common-dir as watch targets; is_interesting_fs_event filters notify events to relevant change kinds before debounced re-emission.

nodes.name: GitWatchState
nodes.summary: Managed Tauri state holding active filesystem watchers keyed by repo root.

nodes.name: resolve_git_watch_targets
nodes.summary: Resolves the set of paths (git-dir and git-common-dir) that should be watched for a repo root.

nodes.name: git_watch_root
nodes.summary: Tauri command that installs a debounced filesystem watcher on a repo's git targets and emits GitStateChanged events on interesting changes.

nodes.name: git_unwatch_root
nodes.summary: Tauri command that tears down and removes the filesystem watcher for a repo root.

nodes.name: worktrees.rs
nodes.summary: Git worktree lifecycle commands: list/add/remove worktrees, orphan/markerless cleanup, prune, and branch enumeration, with a pure porcelain parser and gitdir-marker normalization for managed worktrees.
nodes.languageNotes: parse_worktrees_porcelain reads git worktree list --porcelain into Worktree records (main flag, locked, created_at); remove_orphan and is_managed_markerless_worktree recover from worktrees whose gitdir marker has been deleted.

nodes.name: Worktree
nodes.summary: Serde model describing a git worktree (path, branch, head, main flag, locked state, created-at timestamp).

nodes.name: BranchInfo
nodes.summary: Serde model for a branch listing entry (name plus current-branch flag).

nodes.name: git_worktrees
nodes.summary: Tauri command listing all worktrees for a project via git worktree list --porcelain and parsing them into Worktree records.

nodes.name: parse_worktrees_porcelain
nodes.summary: Pure parser turning git worktree list --porcelain output into Worktree records, deriving main/locked flags and a created-at timestamp.

nodes.name: git_worktree_add
nodes.summary: Tauri command creating a new worktree (existing or new branch from a base) and returning the refreshed worktree list.

nodes.name: git_worktree_remove
nodes.summary: Tauri command removing a worktree (optionally forced) and returning the refreshed list.

nodes.name: git_worktree_remove_orphan
nodes.summary: Recovers and removes a worktree whose gitdir marker has been deleted, including managed markerless directories, then prunes stale administrative entries.

nodes.name: git_branch_list
nodes.summary: Tauri command enumerating local branches via for-each-ref and flagging the current branch.

nodes.name: canvas.zoom.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest suite verifying the ShellCanvas re-emits a PTY resize when the UI zoom changes, mocking Tauri invoke and ResizeObserver to assert shell_resize calls.

nodes.name: terminal.zoom.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest suite ensuring the Terminal panel recomputes geometry on UI zoom changes, with a mocked ResizeObserver and viewport helpers.

nodes.name: useBootConfig.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that loads persisted theme, config.toml settings, and saved UI zoom at startup, applying them to the document and seeding the app state store before the frontend signals ready.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large effect coordinating Promise.all over persisted state and config, seeding many refs (share mode, notify thresholds, update channel) plus optimistic setState writes.

nodes.name: useZoomAndTheme.ts
nodes.summary: React hook exposing zoom adjust/reset and theme-switch handlers that persist to state, update CSS variables, and mirror the boot theme for the next launch.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Application entry point that applies the boot theme as early as possible before the React app mounts.

nodes.name: themeBootstrap.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite covering theme id normalization, legacy id migration, and the boot theme seed/mirror localStorage round-trip.

nodes.name: themeBootstrap.ts
nodes.summary: Pre-React theme utilities: normalizes/migrates legacy theme ids, reads a boot seed from localStorage or the OS color-scheme preference, and applies it to the document root to avoid a flash of unstyled theme.
nodes.languageNotes: Synchronous DOM/localStorage access intended to run before the React tree paints; LEGACY_THEME_MAP migrates older saved ids.

nodes.name: viewport.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite for viewport helpers, validating sane-size clamping, CSS custom-property writes, UI scale application, and zoom readback with stubbed DOM properties.

nodes.name: viewport.ts
nodes.summary: Viewport and UI-scale helpers that compute sane viewport dimensions, write CSS custom properties for zoom compensation, apply a UI scale, and read the current zoom factor from the document root.
nodes.languageNotes: Writes the --app-ui-scale token plus viewport size vars so zoomed chrome stays within the window; dispatches a window event when the scale changes meaningfully.

nodes.name: useBootConfig
nodes.summary: Hook body that resolves persisted theme, config.toml values, and saved zoom on mount, applies them to the DOM, seeds configuration refs, and flips boot-config-ready once complete.

nodes.name: useZoomAndTheme
nodes.summary: Hook body wiring window resize handling and exposing adjustZoom/resetZoom/setTheme callbacks that persist UI scale and theme selection.

nodes.name: readBootThemeSeed
nodes.summary: Reads the saved theme id from localStorage and normalizes it, falling back to the OS light/dark preference when none is stored.

nodes.name: viewportSize
nodes.summary: Resolves a sane viewport dimension for a given axis, preferring visualViewport and falling back through layout measurements.

nodes.name: writeUiViewportVars
nodes.summary: Writes the current viewport width/height and UI scale into CSS custom properties so zoom-compensating containers stay sized to the window.

nodes.name: applyUiScale
nodes.summary: Applies a UI scale factor by setting the --app-ui-scale token and viewport vars, dispatching a window event when the scale changes meaningfully.

nodes.name: readZoom
nodes.summary: Parses the current UI zoom factor from the --app-ui-scale custom property on the document root, defaulting when unset or invalid.

nodes.name: issue-task.ts
nodes.summary: Builds agent task payloads from GitHub issues: assembles the issue prompt, derives a conventional-commit-style git branch (with collision uniquification and length compaction), and interpolates per-project issue templates.

nodes.name: buildIssuePrompt
nodes.summary: Composes a Markdown prompt instructing the agent to work on a specific GitHub issue, embedding the number, title, URL, author, and body.

nodes.name: buildIssueTask
nodes.summary: Top-level task assembler that selects a template, derives a unique branch, and interpolates branch/prompt templates to produce the structured task forwarded to the agent.

nodes.name: interpolateIssueTemplate
nodes.summary: Replaces {{var}} placeholders in a template string with values from the supplied variables map.

nodes.name: issueTemplateVariables
nodes.summary: Builds the variable map exposed to issue templates, including issue metadata, derived branch, slug, labels, and project context.

nodes.name: buildIssueBranch
nodes.summary: Derives a git branch name from an issue (type prefix + slug), compacting overlong names and falling back to a timestamp suffix when collisions persist.

nodes.name: compactIssueBranch
nodes.summary: Truncates a branch name to a maximum length while protecting the prefix and trimming on a word boundary where possible.

nodes.name: classifyIssueBranch
nodes.summary: Parses a conventional-commit-style scope/type from the issue title and combines it with a label-derived prefix to classify the branch.

nodes.name: branchPrefixFor
nodes.summary: Maps a normalized commit type token to the canonical git branch prefix (feat, fix, docs, chore, etc.).

nodes.name: branchPrefixForLabels
nodes.summary: Infers a branch prefix from issue labels by regex-matching label names against bug, docs, ci, test, perf, refactor, chore, and feature categories.

nodes.name: issue-templates.ts
nodes.summary: Loads and normalizes per-project issue-task templates from disk via a Tauri invoke, and selects the templates matching a given issue's labels.

nodes.name: loadIssueTemplates
nodes.summary: Reads the project's issue-template config through a Tauri invoke and normalizes it, returning an empty config with a warning on failure.

nodes.name: normalizeIssueTemplatesConfig
nodes.summary: Coerces raw config input into a validated templates array plus optional warning, dropping malformed template entries.

nodes.name: normalizeIssueTemplate
nodes.summary: Validates a single raw template object, trimming fields and filtering label conditions, returning null when required fields are missing.

nodes.name: matchingIssueTemplates
nodes.summary: Returns templates applicable to an issue, ordering label-specific matches ahead of catch-all templates.

nodes.name: issues-section.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the IssuesSection dashboard component, mocking the issues cache and Tauri invoke to verify rendering, refresh, context-menu actions, and template-driven task dispatch.

nodes.name: issues-section.tsx
nodes.summary: React component rendering the dashboard's GitHub issues list, with lazy load on visibility, refresh, context menu, and actions that dispatch issue-derived tasks to the agent in the current or a new workspace.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses IntersectionObserver-gated lazy loading and JSON Pointer $ref resolution against the shared A2UI state store.

nodes.name: IssuesSection
nodes.summary: The main issues-panel React component: manages load/refresh state, the right-click context menu, template selection, and dispatching issue tasks to the agent via onEvent.

nodes.name: projectWorkspaceBranches
nodes.summary: Collects the existing branch and label names across a project's workspaces from the sidebar state, used to avoid branch-name collisions.

nodes.name: currentProjectWorkspaceId
nodes.summary: Resolves the active workspace id for a project from sidebar state, preferring the active workspace and falling back to the main workspace.

nodes.name: ghIssuesCache.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory TTL cache (90s live, 60s negative) over the gh_issue_list Tauri command, with in-flight request de-duplication, force-refresh, peek, and per-issue detail fetch.
nodes.languageNotes: De-duplicates concurrent fetches via an in-flight promise map keyed by project+limit.

nodes.name: getIssues
nodes.summary: Returns cached issues when fresh, otherwise fetches via gh_issue_list, coalescing concurrent calls and caching empty results on error to avoid hammering gh.

nodes.name: refreshIssues
nodes.summary: Evicts the cache entry for a project/limit and re-fetches issues, forcing a fresh gh call.

nodes.name: getIssueDetail
nodes.summary: Fetches full detail for a single issue number via the gh_issue_detail Tauri command.

nodes.name: ghIssuesCache.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the GitHub issues cache verifying TTL behavior, in-flight de-duplication, force refresh, and error fallback against a mocked Tauri invoke.

nodes.name: makeIssue
nodes.summary: Test factory producing a GhIssue fixture with overridable fields.

nodes.name: time.ts
nodes.summary: Provides formatRelativeTime, converting an epoch-millisecond timestamp into a human-friendly relative string (now, minutes, hours, days) or a locale date.

nodes.name: formatRelativeTime
nodes.summary: Formats an epoch-millisecond timestamp as a relative time string, falling back to a locale date string beyond a week.

nodes.name: time.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for formatRelativeTime covering the now, minutes, hours, days, and locale-date boundaries.

nodes.name: io.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite exercising the local-chat append/prune/truncate I/O helpers, asserting the on-disk JSONL chat file stays bounded and consistent.

nodes.name: io.ts
nodes.summary: Filesystem I/O for session history: reads/writes the human-readable session label and appends, prunes, and truncates the local-chat JSONL transcript that mirrors pi sessions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses node:fs/promises with atomic rename-after-write (tmp file + rename) when rewriting the chat file to avoid partial writes.

nodes.name: lookup.ts
nodes.summary: Locates the pi session log whose recorded cwd matches an expected project directory, canonicalizing paths via realpath and reading only the session-header line for efficiency.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads just the header bytes via a fixed-size buffer (open/read/close) rather than loading the whole session log, with a realpath cache keyed on canonical cwd.

nodes.name: metadata.ts
nodes.summary: Derives session summary metadata (cwd, first-message preview, label, validity) from the latest session log in a session directory for listing recent sessions.

nodes.name: parse-local.ts
nodes.summary: Parses the local-chat JSONL transcript into restored chat messages, filtering by expected cwd, de-duplicating by id, and reconstructing attachments and A2UI flags.

nodes.name: restore.ts
nodes.summary: Reconstructs a tab's full chat transcript by merging pi session messages with the local-chat mirror — deduping covered content, tool cards, and compaction markers, merging attachments, and ordering chronologically.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy reconciliation layer: content/tool-card/compaction coverage detection plus attachment merging and chronological interleave keep the pi log authoritative while recovering local-only context.

nodes.name: shared.ts
nodes.summary: Shared constants, types, and pure helpers for the session-history module — chat-role guards, attachment parsing, text/thinking extraction from content blocks, and cwd/text normalization.

nodes.name: readSessionLabel
nodes.summary: Reads and normalizes the optional human-set label for a session directory, returning undefined when absent or empty.

nodes.name: writeSessionLabel
nodes.summary: Persists or clears a session's custom label, normalizing it and unlinking the label file when empty.

nodes.name: appendLocalChatMessage
nodes.summary: Validates and appends a chat message to the local-chat JSONL file, assigning ids/timestamps and triggering a prune to keep the file bounded.

nodes.name: pruneLocalChatFile
nodes.summary: Trims the local-chat file to the most recent MAX_LOCAL_CHAT_MESSAGES entries by parsing, slicing, and atomically rewriting it.

nodes.name: truncateLocalChatAfterEntry
nodes.summary: Drops local-chat entries created at or after a cutoff timestamp and rewrites the file, used when rolling back or forking a session.

nodes.name: canonicalCwd
nodes.summary: Resolves a path to its canonical form via realpath with trailing-slash stripping and a memoization cache, falling back to the lexical path on error.

nodes.name: readSessionHeaderCwd
nodes.summary: Reads just the leading bytes of a session log and parses the header line to extract the recorded cwd without loading the entire file.

nodes.name: findSessionFileMatchingCwd
nodes.summary: Scans a session directory for the session log whose canonicalized header cwd matches an expected directory, returning the most recent match.

nodes.name: metaFromSessionLines
nodes.summary: Extracts cwd and a first-user-message preview from raw session log lines to build summary metadata for a session.

nodes.name: latestSessionLog
nodes.summary: Finds the most recently modified session log file within a session directory.

nodes.name: readSessionMetadata
nodes.summary: Assembles a session's summary metadata from its latest log plus stored label, validating that the recorded cwd still exists as a directory.

nodes.name: parseLocalChatLines
nodes.summary: Parses local-chat JSONL lines into restored chat messages, filtering by expected cwd, de-duplicating by id, and reconstructing text, attachments, and A2UI flags.

nodes.name: readLocalChatTranscript
nodes.summary: Reads the local-chat file from a session directory and parses it into restored messages, returning an empty list when absent.

nodes.name: isCoveredByPiContent
nodes.summary: Determines whether a local message's text content is already represented in the pi content index so duplicates can be dropped during merge.

nodes.name: toolCardRecords
nodes.summary: Extracts tool-card identity records (name + normalized id) from a restored message's tool invocations.

nodes.name: removePiToolCardsCoveredByLocalCancellation
nodes.summary: Filters out pi tool-card messages whose tool calls were locally cancelled, so the local cancellation card wins during restore.

nodes.name: isCompactionMarker
nodes.summary: Detects whether a message is a context-compaction marker by matching normalized known marker prefixes.

nodes.name: isCoveredByPiCompaction
nodes.summary: Checks whether a local compaction marker is already present in the pi messages within a time window so it is not duplicated.

nodes.name: dedupeLocalMessages
nodes.summary: Filters local-only messages down to those not already covered by pi content, tool cards, or compaction markers.

nodes.name: mergeAttachments
nodes.summary: Merges two attachment lists, de-duplicating by a composite key over kind, id, path, name, mime type, and size.

nodes.name: findPiMessageForLocalAttachments
nodes.summary: Finds the closest matching pi message (same content, nearest timestamp) onto which a local message's attachments should be merged.

nodes.name: mergeLocalAttachmentsIntoPiMessages
nodes.summary: Folds attachments from local messages into their matching pi messages so restored pi content carries the local-only attachments.

nodes.name: mergeRestoredMessagesChronologically
nodes.summary: Interleaves pi and local-only messages into a single chronological list, ordering by createdAt and falling back to source order for ties.

nodes.name: readSessionTranscript
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that locates the right session log, parses pi history, merges in the local-chat mirror, and returns the bounded chronological transcript for restore.

nodes.name: parseChatAttachments
nodes.summary: Parses and validates raw attachment records from persisted chat entries into typed ChatAttachment objects, discarding malformed entries.

nodes.name: textFromContent
nodes.summary: Extracts the visible text from a message content value, handling both plain strings and arrays of typed content blocks.

nodes.name: thinkingFromContent
nodes.summary: Extracts concatenated reasoning/thinking text from an array of content blocks, returning undefined when none is present.

nodes.name: aethonControl.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite for the aethonctl CLI helpers, exercising account-switch payload construction, tab normalization, JSON Pointer reads, skill install planning, and the control/debug transport clients against an in-process socket server.
nodes.languageNotes: Spins up a real node:net socket server in a temp dir to test AethonControlClient end-to-end rather than mocking the transport.

nodes.name: aethonControl.ts
nodes.summary: Core library backing the aethonctl CLI: JSON Pointer state reads, tab normalization, two-step account-switch payload assembly, skill markdown generation/install planning, and high-level agent operations (chat send, open/close tab, wait-until-idle) over a control or debug client.
nodes.languageNotes: Transport-agnostic helpers take a client object so the same logic drives both the release control socket and the debug eval transport.

nodes.name: aethonctl.ts
nodes.summary: Executable entry point for the aethonctl command-line tool: parses global flags, selects a control/debug transport, and dispatches the tabs, accounts, chat, agent, skills, state, and eval subcommands against a running Aethon instance.
nodes.languageNotes: Bun shebang (#!/usr/bin/env bun) with a hand-rolled argv parser and a transport mode of auto/control/debug; --wait ceiling defaults to 30 minutes to avoid false timeouts on long agent turns.

nodes.name: controlClient.ts
nodes.summary: Release-safe transport client that connects to the Aethon control Unix/TCP socket, reads the token-authenticated control-info file, and issues newline-delimited JSON request/response calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses node:net createConnection with a timeout guard and a newline-delimited JSON framing protocol; token is read from a sidecar file referenced by the control-info JSON.

nodes.name: debugClient.ts
nodes.summary: Debug-build transport client that resolves the dev debug port from dev-info.json (including sandbox dirs), then ships JavaScript to the webview TCP eval server via debug-eval.sh for eval/evalJson/invoke calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Discovers the running dev instance by scanning ${TMPDIR}/aethon-dev sandbox directories for dev-info.json, mirroring scripts/dev.sh's port-write behavior.

nodes.name: jsonPointerGet
nodes.summary: Resolves a JSON Pointer path against a state value, walking child keys with RFC 6901 ~1/~0 unescaping.

nodes.name: buildAccountSwitchPayloads
nodes.summary: Constructs the two-step auth_profile_use_for_tab + auth_profile_apply payload pair the UI uses to switch a tab worker's account, matching the frontend's account-switch contract.

nodes.name: skillMarkdown
nodes.summary: Generates the markdown body for an aethonctl-installed Claude Code skill file given a command name.

nodes.name: planSkillInstall
nodes.summary: Builds an install plan for a skill across the requested project/home targets, expanding target aliases and rendering the destination paths and markdown contents.

nodes.name: expandSkillTargets
nodes.summary: Expands requested skill-install targets (project/home/all) into concrete deduplicated target keys, probing the filesystem for existing skill directories.

nodes.name: installSkill
nodes.summary: Writes a planned skill file to disk, creating parent directories and refusing to overwrite an existing file unless force is set.

nodes.name: fetchSnapshot
nodes.summary: Fetches the current Aethon webview state snapshot via the client's evalJson transport for downstream JSON Pointer reads.

nodes.name: activeTargetFromState
nodes.summary: Derives the active tab target (id, cwd, model) from a state snapshot, honoring an explicitly requested tab when provided.

nodes.name: applyAccountSwitch
nodes.summary: Applies an account switch for a target tab by invoking the two-step auth-profile payloads through the client.

nodes.name: sendChat
nodes.summary: Sends a chat message to the agent for a given tab via the client invoke transport, threading model and options.

nodes.name: openAgentTab
nodes.summary: Opens a new agent tab with project/model/account options by evaluating tab-creation JS in the webview, then applies any requested account switch.

nodes.name: closeAgentTab
nodes.summary: Closes an agent tab by id by evaluating tab-removal JS in the webview.

nodes.name: waitUntilIdle
nodes.summary: Polls the webview state until the agent turn completes or a timeout elapses, returning the idle/timeout outcome.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Top-level command dispatcher: parses global options, resolves the runtime/client, and routes to the tabs/accounts/chat/agent/skills/state/eval/raw subcommand handlers.

nodes.name: parseGlobal
nodes.summary: Hand-rolled argv parser extracting global flags (json, port, socket, transport) and returning the residual positional args.

nodes.name: createRuntime
nodes.summary: Builds the runtime client(s) for the selected transport mode, wiring control and/or debug clients with the resolved port/socket.

nodes.name: runTabs
nodes.summary: Handles the `tabs` subcommand family (list/open/close) by reading snapshots and invoking the open/close agent-tab helpers.

nodes.name: runAccounts
nodes.summary: Handles the `accounts` subcommand: lists auth profiles or applies a two-step account switch to the active/target tab.

nodes.name: runChat
nodes.summary: Handles the `chat` subcommand: resolves the target tab, optionally switches account, sends a message, and waits for the turn to go idle.

nodes.name: runAgent
nodes.summary: Handles the `agent` subcommand: forwards raw agent commands via the debug invoke/eval transport and reports the active target.

nodes.name: runSkills
nodes.summary: Handles the `skills` subcommand: previews skill markdown or plans and installs aethonctl skill files to the requested targets.

nodes.name: printHelp
nodes.summary: Prints the aethonctl usage/help text covering global flags and all subcommands.

nodes.name: defaultControlInfoPath
nodes.summary: Resolves the default path to the control-info JSON under the Aethon user directory.

nodes.name: readControlInfo
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the control-info JSON (socket/port + token path) if it exists, returning null otherwise.

nodes.name: AethonControlClient
nodes.summary: Control-socket client: loads the token-authenticated control-info, opens a node:net connection, and performs timeout-guarded newline-delimited JSON request/response exchanges.
nodes.languageNotes: Frames each request as a single JSON line terminated by newline and reads until the first newline in the response buffer.

nodes.name: resolveDevInfoPath
nodes.summary: Locates dev-info.json by checking the standard user dir and scanning sandbox dev directories, returning the freshest match.

nodes.name: resolveDebugPort
nodes.summary: Resolves the debug eval-server port from an explicit option or by reading dev-info.json.

nodes.name: invokeJs
nodes.summary: Builds the JavaScript snippet that calls a Tauri invoke command with serialized args for evaluation in the webview.

nodes.name: AethonDebugClient
nodes.summary: Debug-build client that ships JavaScript to the webview TCP eval server (via debug-eval.sh) and exposes eval/evalJson/invoke helpers with ERROR-prefix unwrapping.
nodes.languageNotes: Delegates the actual eval transport to execFile of scripts/debug-eval.sh; evalJson/invoke detect an ERROR: prefix in stdout and reject accordingly.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Arch Linux (base-devel) build container that installs the Tauri/webkit2gtk toolchain plus bun and rustup, creates a passwordless sudo 'builder' user, and runs scripts/aur-build-all.sh as its entrypoint to build and test the AUR package.
nodes.languageNotes: Parameterizes the base image platform via the AUR_PLATFORM build arg so the same Dockerfile builds for linux/amd64 (default) or other targets. Disables the pacman build sandbox and drops to an unprivileged builder user with NOPASSWD sudo, the standard makepkg-in-container pattern for AUR builds.

nodes.name: _ci.yml
nodes.summary: Reusable workflow_call CI gate defining the single source-of-truth lint, typecheck, and test jobs (TS, Playwright e2e, Rust) consumed by ci.yml and release-please.yml.
nodes.languageNotes: Reusable workflow invoked via `on: workflow_call`; runs no secrets so callers omit `secrets: inherit`. ts and rust jobs use an os matrix (ubuntu-24.04 + macos-15).

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Top-level CI trigger that fires on push/PR to main and delegates all checks to the reusable _ci.yml workflow, with cancel-in-progress concurrency per ref.
nodes.languageNotes: Thin caller — its single job uses `./.github/workflows/_ci.yml`, keeping the check definitions in exactly one place.

nodes.name: copilot-review.yml
nodes.summary: Requests a GitHub Copilot review on every non-draft PR by assigning the Copilot reviewer via REST and posting an `@copilot review` comment as a reliable trigger.
nodes.languageNotes: Belt-and-braces dual trigger: REST `requested_reviewers` plus a PR comment, since the REST assignment alone has not reliably activated Copilot.

nodes.name: docs.yml
nodes.summary: Builds the VitePress user guide under website/ on every PR/push and deploys it to GitHub Pages on main only, with separate concurrency groups for build and deploy.
nodes.languageNotes: Two jobs: build (path-filtered on website/**) and deploy (needs build, main-only, github-pages environment with non-cancelling concurrency to protect in-flight deploys).

nodes.name: nightly.yml
nodes.summary: Nightly macOS build pipeline chained off green CI via workflow_run; computes a dev version, builds a signed Tauri bundle into a staging release, then atomically promotes staging to the live nightly release.
nodes.languageNotes: Triggered by `workflow_run` after the CI workflow succeeds on main; resolves the tested head_sha explicitly. Build has a 3-attempt continue-on-error retry pattern to absorb Apple-notarization flakes; publish promotes nightly-staging → nightly with a ~1-2s blackout window.

nodes.name: release-please.yml
nodes.summary: Release automation that runs release-please to manage the release PR, re-syncs version files, re-runs the CI gate, builds and signs the macOS Tauri bundle, publishes the GitHub release, and publishes the aethon crate to crates.io.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a short-lived GitHub App token so the release PR is authored by a real identity and CI fires (default GITHUB_TOKEN would be suppressed by GitHub's recursion guard). Embeds the reusable _ci.yml as a gate before the signed build; build uses the same 3-attempt notarization retry.

nodes.name: ts
nodes.summary: Matrix job (ubuntu + macos) that installs Bun deps, verifies version-file sync, then runs TypeScript typecheck (tsc -b), ESLint, and Vitest.

nodes.name: e2e
nodes.summary: Ubuntu job that installs Playwright Chromium with deps and runs the end-to-end test suite via bun run test:e2e.

nodes.name: rust
nodes.summary: Matrix job (ubuntu + macos) that installs the pinned Rust 1.92 toolchain and Linux build deps, then runs cargo clippy (deny warnings) and cargo test --lib.

nodes.name: request-review
nodes.summary: Skips drafts, then requests Copilot as a reviewer via the REST requested_reviewers endpoint and posts an `@copilot review` PR comment to reliably trigger the review.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Installs website/ Bun deps and builds the VitePress site; on main, configures Pages and uploads the dist artifact for deployment.

nodes.name: deploy
nodes.summary: Main-only job that deploys the uploaded Pages artifact to the github-pages environment via actions/deploy-pages.

nodes.name: compute-version
nodes.summary: Resolves the CI-tested head SHA and derives a nightly dev version (NEXT-dev.<commits>.g<shortsha>) from the open release-please PR or a patch-bump of the release manifest.

nodes.name: prepare-release
nodes.summary: Resets the draft nightly-staging GitHub release with generated notes so the live nightly release stays available to the updater during the build.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Stamps the nightly version into package.json, syncs it across version files, and builds a signed/notarized macOS aarch64 Tauri bundle into nightly-staging with a 3-attempt retry.

nodes.name: publish
nodes.summary: Builds the updater latest.json against the future nightly URL, then atomically promotes nightly-staging to the live nightly release and smoke-tests anonymous manifest reachability.

nodes.name: release-please
nodes.summary: Mints a GitHub App token and runs release-please-action to open/update the release PR and create releases, marking new releases as draft until artifacts upload.

nodes.name: sync-version-files
nodes.summary: On the release PR branch, runs bun run version:sync to propagate package.json into tauri.conf.json/Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock and pushes the result as the App identity so CI fires on the final HEAD.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Checks out the resolved tag and builds the signed/notarized macOS aarch64 Tauri release bundle with a 3-attempt notarization retry, gated behind the CI gate.

nodes.name: publish
nodes.summary: Builds the updater latest.json from the release .sig assets, uploads it, and un-drafts the GitHub release.

nodes.name: publish-crate
nodes.summary: On stable v-tag releases, installs the Rust toolchain plus Linux build deps and publishes the aethon crate to crates.io via cargo publish --locked.

nodes.name: debug-agent-diag.sh
nodes.summary: Read-only aethon-debug helper that dumps the agent-worker diagnostics table (one row per live aethon-agent worker: key, tab id, cwd, pid, idle/activity timing, prompt-in-flight) by invoking the agent_diagnostics Tauri command.

nodes.name: debug-agent-new-tab.sh
nodes.summary: Creates an agent tab pinned to a chosen cwd by both pushing a tab entry into the webview central store and sending a tab_open message to the agent bridge, mirroring the header's New Tab gesture without needing the React tab strip in a particular state.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds a JS snippet via a heredoc and execs debug-eval.sh; drives both the central state store (__AETHON_SET_STATE__) and the agent bridge (agent_command tab_open).

nodes.name: debug-chat-send.sh
nodes.summary: Sends a chat message to an agent tab the same way pressing Enter in the composer would, JSON-encoding the message/mode/tabId and forwarding to the send_message Tauri command via debug-invoke.sh.

nodes.name: debug-chat-wait.sh
nodes.summary: Polls a tab via webview state until the agent finishes its current response (waiting flag false AND message count advanced past the captured baseline), then prints the latest assistant message and tool-call summary.

nodes.name: debug-devshell.sh
nodes.summary: Inspects and manipulates the Nix devshell cache from the skill, dispatching status/env/refresh subcommands to the devshell_status, devshell_env_for_path, and devshell_refresh Tauri commands, resolving the project root from active-project state when none is given.

nodes.name: debug-eval.sh
nodes.summary: Core aethon-debug primitive that ships arbitrary JavaScript to the running dev app's TCP debug server (127.0.0.1:19433) for webview eval, with multi-strategy port discovery (env override, dev-info.json, dev --new sandbox glob, default) and auto-start of the dev build when it is not yet running.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds Python3 socket clients for connectivity probes and the eval round-trip; resolves the debug port by mtime-newest sandbox dev-info.json and tolerates a cold dev build by nohup-launching dev and polling until the server binds.

nodes.name: debug-invoke.sh
nodes.summary: Generic Tauri-command invoker that wraps __TAURI_INTERNALS__.invoke(command, args) into a JS snippet and runs it through debug-eval.sh, serving as the lowest-level command bridge under the higher-level gesture scripts.

nodes.name: debug-logs.sh
nodes.summary: Tails or greps the on-disk logs Aethon writes to ~/.aethon/logs/ across both the Rust (aethon.YYYY-MM-DD) and bun bridge (bridge.YYYY-MM-DD.log) series, with follow, source filtering, line-count, since-day, and pattern options.

nodes.name: debug-screenshot.sh
nodes.summary: Captures a window-scoped screenshot of the Aethon dev app: on macOS it raises the dev process by PID (never by app name) via System Events, reads the front window's bounds, and region-captures it with screencapture, falling back to full-display or Linux grim/scrot/import when bounds are unavailable.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves the GUI PID via lsof on the debug port (the listener is the window we want) and uses an osascript heredoc bound by unix id to avoid name-based activation of a stale release .app; optional sips downscale for docs assets.

nodes.name: debug-shell-close.sh
nodes.summary: Closes a shell tab (mirroring the user clicking the X on the tab strip) by JSON-encoding the tabId and invoking the shell_close Tauri command via debug-invoke.sh.

nodes.name: debug-shell-read.sh
nodes.summary: Dumps a shell tab's recent scrollback regardless of share mode via the dev-only debug_shell_snapshot command, returning the raw JSON snapshot or, in raw mode, just the extracted tail bytes.

nodes.name: debug-shell-spawn.sh
nodes.summary: Opens a shell tab as if the user clicked + new shell, resolving cwd from active-project state and invoking shell_open (which fires the devshell wrap), so UAT exercises the real user gesture rather than a Rust-only shortcut.

nodes.name: debug-shell-write.sh
nodes.summary: Sends raw input to a shell tab via the dev-only debug_shell_write_raw command, bypassing the production share-mode gate and auto-appending a newline so command lines actually execute.

nodes.name: debug-wait.sh
nodes.summary: Blocks until Aethon's global state.waiting flips false (agent done responding) by repeatedly eval'ing a state snapshot through debug-eval.sh, returning a JSON snapshot on completion or a timeout sentinel.

nodes.name: resolve_root
nodes.summary: Resolves the target project root, returning the explicit argument when given or else eval'ing webview state to read the active project's path (falling back to projectRoot/aethonRoot).

nodes.name: resolve_dev_info
nodes.summary: Locates dev-info.json, preferring the conventional ~/.aethon path and otherwise selecting the most-recently-modified dev --new sandbox file so the skill works across both launch modes.

nodes.name: resolve_port
nodes.summary: Determines the debug server port from the AETHON_DEBUG_PORT override, then a re-resolved dev-info.json's debugPort field, falling back to the built-in default 19433.

nodes.name: server_up
nodes.summary: Probes whether the debug server is reachable on a given port by attempting a 1-second TCP connect from an embedded Python3 socket client.

nodes.name: ensure_app_running
nodes.summary: Ensures the dev build is up before eval: returns the port if the server already responds, otherwise nohup-launches dev (or bun tauri dev), then polls until the debug server binds or the start timeout elapses.

nodes.name: resolve_dev_info
nodes.summary: Resolves dev-info.json, preferring the conventional path and otherwise picking the newest dev --new sandbox file by mtime.

nodes.name: resolve_pid
nodes.summary: Resolves the dev GUI PID, preferring the process LISTENing on the debug port (via lsof, since the debug server runs inside the Tauri window) and only falling back to dev-info.json's launcher pid as a last resort.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context exclusion list that keeps node_modules, build artifacts, target/, and local state out of the image build, used by the AUR/packaging Dockerfile.

nodes.name: .release-please-manifest.json
nodes.summary: release-please version manifest tracking the current released version of the root package, updated automatically as part of the release PR flow.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive 38-section agent guidance document covering the project stack, architecture layers, frontend model, runtime API, terminal panel, agent runtime contract, and build notes for AI coding agents working in the repo.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Release changelog spanning versions 0.1.0 through 0.9.0, mixing release-please generated entries with hand-curated Keep-a-Changelog sections covering features, fixes, and breaking changes.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project guidance for Claude Code covering the Tauri/React/agent stack, common commands, the three-layer architecture, frontend model, agent process concurrency, devshell wrapping, and Nix build notes.

nodes.name: DESIGN.md
nodes.summary: Aethon design system specification defining the Ember Brass color palette, typography hierarchy, elevation/shadow vocabulary, component recipes, and the signature agent-canvas and terminal surfaces.

nodes.name: PRODUCT.md
nodes.summary: Product brief describing the target users, product purpose, brand personality, anti-references, design principles, and accessibility goals for Aethon.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project entry-point README covering what Aethon does, getting started, the Nix package and devshell commands, versioning, architecture overview, project layout, and releasing.

nodes.name: RELEASING.md
nodes.summary: Release operations guide detailing the stable and nightly release flows, Tauri signing key and Apple notarization one-time setup, local signed builds, and CI signing verification.

nodes.name: SPEC.md
nodes.summary: Design specification and milestone checklist (M1 through M7) capturing the vision, layered architecture, A2UI integration, extension model, theming, packaging, and configuration decisions for Aethon.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point that mounts the React app into #root via /src/main.tsx and preloads the Geist, Inter, JetBrains Mono, and Playfair Display web fonts.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend/agent package manifest defining the aethonctl bin, dev/build/test/lint scripts, and the React 19, Tauri API, pi-coding-agent, Monaco, xterm, and Vite/Vitest dependency set.
nodes.languageNotes: ESM package ("type": "module"); build script chains version:check, tsc -b, and vite build.

nodes.name: release-please-config.json
nodes.summary: release-please configuration setting the release-type, pre-major minor bumping, custom changelog sections, and the tracked package list for automated release PRs.

nodes.name: rust-toolchain.toml
nodes.summary: Rust toolchain pin for non-Nix builds; the Nix devshell pin in flake.nix overrides this inside the dev environment.

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project-reference config for the React frontend source, compiling src/** with bundler module resolution and strict settings for the Vite app.

nodes.name: tsconfig.e2e.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project-reference config scoped to the Playwright end-to-end test sources under e2e/.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript solution config that owns no files and composes the app, node, and e2e project references for tsc -b build mode.
nodes.languageNotes: Solution-style tsconfig: empty files array plus references array drives composite project builds via tsc -b.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project-reference config for Node-context build tooling (Vite/Playwright config and scripts), separate from the browser app compilation unit.

nodes.name: nix.instructions.md
nodes.summary: Path-scoped Copilot review-instruction file for Nix and devshell code, codifying the Rust toolchain pin, platform linker/webkit workarounds, formatting style, and devshell helper expectations.
nodes.languageNotes: GitHub Copilot custom instructions document (applyTo-scoped) that drives automated PR review guidance rather than runtime behavior.

nodes.name: rust.instructions.md
nodes.summary: Path-scoped Copilot review-instruction file for the Rust/Tauri shell, covering shell-only logic boundaries, concurrency, filesystem path safety, plugin additions, debug-only gating, logging, and clippy expectations.
nodes.languageNotes: GitHub Copilot custom instructions document scoped to Rust sources to steer automated review of the thin Tauri shell.

nodes.name: tests.instructions.md
nodes.summary: Path-scoped Copilot review-instruction file for tests, defining colocated test layout, Vitest expectations, coverage-as-signal philosophy, anti-patterns to flag, determinism rules, and Rust test conventions.
nodes.languageNotes: GitHub Copilot custom instructions document scoped to test files to guide automated review of test quality and determinism.

nodes.name: typescript.instructions.md
nodes.summary: Path-scoped Copilot review-instruction file for TypeScript/React code, covering the strict-mode contract, React 19 patterns, A2UI rendering rules, event routing precedence, and agent-bridge conventions.
nodes.languageNotes: GitHub Copilot custom instructions document scoped to TS/React sources, encoding the project's strict-mode and A2UI rendering conventions for automated review.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index for the bundled Aethon agent reference docs (shipped at $AETHON_DOCS_DIR), pointing the model at api.md, components.md, extensions.md, and project-config.md and explaining how to introspect live UI state.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown table mapping each reference doc to its purpose; explicitly instructs the agent to read these on demand rather than from memory.

nodes.name: api.md
nodes.summary: Authoritative reference for the globalThis.aethon runtime API and agent-visible A2UI tools (setState, setLayout, patchLayout, canvas/windows mutations, registerComponent/Theme/Keybinding/Layout, notify, sessions, shells, editor, tasks, dashboard) plus lifecycle, event handling, and introspection helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Large (~900 non-empty lines, 39 sections) API reference organized by namespace with worked code examples for each tool.

nodes.name: components.md
nodes.summary: Catalog of A2UI primitive components (text, card, button, container, code, table, form, for-each, etc.) and extension-provided composites (layout, sidebar, tab-strip, terminal-panel, dashboards), including prop schemas, the $ref JSON-Pointer data-binding contract, layout-slot contract, and window-event channels.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest doc in the set (50 sections); documents the override-by-type rule and the canonical layout-slot contract used by the renderer.

nodes.name: extensions.md
nodes.summary: Guide to authoring Aethon extensions across the three distribution shapes (loose, project-local, npm-distributed), documenting the extension API surface and eight worked recipes covering themes, sidebar sections, live state updates, layout replacement, native canvas windows, and custom A2UI components.
nodes.languageNotes: Recipe-driven authoring guide with a design-token surface reference and lifecycle/debugging sections.

nodes.name: project-config.md
nodes.summary: Reference for project-local .aethon configuration: workspace startup commands and .aethon/issues.toml issue-to-agent prompt templates, including template fields and substitution variables.

nodes.name: text-selection.md
nodes.summary: Defines the app-wide text-selection policy (which surfaces are selectable vs. interactive chrome) and how to implement it in CSS, with guidance for reviewing changes.

nodes.name: aur-build-all.sh
nodes.summary: Builds every AUR package found under packaging/aur/ by copying each PKGBUILD directory into a clean build root and running makepkg, failing loudly if no packages are found.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses shopt -s nullglob and an array of package dirs so an empty glob is detected explicitly rather than iterating a literal pattern.

nodes.name: build-updater-manifest.sh
nodes.summary: Assembles the Tauri updater manifest (latest.json) from a directory of per-platform .sig files, emitting the version/notes/pub_date/platforms shape that tauri-plugin-updater consumes for the darwin-aarch64 leg.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds the platforms object incrementally with jq (--rawfile for the signature, --argjson to merge accumulated JSON), centralizing manifest construction to avoid the multi-arch parallel-upload race.

nodes.name: dev.sh
nodes.summary: Aethon dev launcher that finds free ports for Vite and the debug TCP server, records them in ~/.aethon/dev-info.json for the aethon-debug skill, then supervises cargo tauri dev through the macOS .app runner with --new sandbox and --clean nuke modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Port auto-increment keeps Vite (strictPort) and the debug server in lockstep so multiple dev instances don't collide; a trap-based cleanup tears down the per-PID tmp sandbox on exit.

nodes.name: macos-dev-app-runner.sh
nodes.summary: Custom cargo-tauri runner for macOS dev builds that wraps the debug binary in a signed Aethon Dev.app and launches it via Launch Services, so macOS TCC attributes microphone and speech-recognition prompts to Aethon rather than the terminal.
nodes.languageNotes: Tracks and terminates launched-app PIDs to keep the dev lifecycle clean; re-execs under bash when invoked by a non-bash shell.

nodes.name: stage-lfm2-runner.sh
nodes.summary: Stages the prebuilt llama-lfm2-audio voice runner into src-tauri/binaries/lfm2-audio/ by downloading a commit+SHA-256-pinned per-platform zip from Hugging Face, verifying the checksum, and code-signing every nested Mach-O for notarization; unsupported platforms skip gracefully.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets up a throwaway signing keychain mirroring Tauri's own keychain dance (import cert, set-key-partition-list, prepend to search list) and restores the original keychain list via a cleanup trap; checksum verification falls back from sha256sum to shasum across platforms.

nodes.name: verify-bundle.sh
nodes.summary: Fail-loud safety net that scans a built Aethon.app (and any staged LFM2-Audio runner) with otool -L for /nix/store paths in Mach-O load commands, aborting the build before a bundle that won't dyld-link on non-builder Macs can ship.
nodes.languageNotes: Greps otool -L output for /nix/store install_names; non-Darwin hosts skip cleanly, and a find -print0 loop checks each nested dylib/so in the bundled runner resources.

nodes.name: ensure_frontend_deps
nodes.summary: Checks whether frontend dependencies are installed and runs bun install when needed, unless AETHON_SKIP_BUN_INSTALL short-circuits the check.

nodes.name: find_free_port
nodes.summary: Scans upward from a base port using is_port_busy to find the first available port, used to keep Vite and the debug server from colliding across concurrent dev instances.

nodes.name: launched_app_pids
nodes.summary: Resolves the running process IDs for the launched Aethon Dev.app so the runner can track and later terminate the app it started.

nodes.name: terminate_app_pids
nodes.summary: Terminates a set of app PIDs gracefully then forcibly, ensuring the wrapped dev app does not leak processes when the dev session ends.

nodes.name: terminate_launched_app
nodes.summary: Tears down the Aethon Dev.app that this runner launched via Launch Services, resolving its PIDs and terminating them.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Rust crate manifest for the Tauri shell — declares the aethon_lib library (staticlib/cdylib/rlib) and aethon binary, pins Tauri 2 plus serde/notify and platform-gated dependencies (macOS, Windows, unix), and defines feature flags and the build script's dev/build dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Rust 2024 edition and target-specific dependency tables (cfg(target_os = "macos"), cfg(windows), cfg(unix)) so OS-boundary crates are only pulled in on the relevant platform.

nodes.name: Entitlements.plist
nodes.summary: macOS code-signing entitlements granting the app audio-input and speech-recognition capabilities required by the voice-to-text composer feature.
nodes.languageNotes: Apple property-list (plist) XML; these entitlements must be embedded at signing time for the sandboxed app to access the microphone and SFSpeechRecognizer.

nodes.name: Info.plist
nodes.summary: macOS app Info.plist supplying the user-facing privacy usage descriptions (NSMicrophoneUsageDescription, NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription) shown in the OS permission prompts for voice input.
nodes.languageNotes: Apple property-list (plist) XML; macOS refuses microphone/speech access at runtime unless these usage-description strings are present in the bundle.

nodes.name: tauri.conf.json
nodes.summary: Top-level Tauri 2 application configuration — sets product identity and version, build hooks (bun dev/build, LFM2 runner staging), the overlay-titlebar main window (initially hidden for window-state restore), bundle targets/resources/icons, macOS entitlements wiring, the bundled aethon-agent external binary, and the GitHub-backed auto-updater endpoint and pubkey.
nodes.languageNotes: Bundles docs and the default-layout A2UI payloads as resources, ships the agent as an externalBin, and references Entitlements.plist/Info.plist for macOS — so this file ties the Rust shell, the agent subprocess, and the frontend layout together at packaging time.

nodes.name: chrome.css
nodes.summary: The application's master chrome stylesheet — roughly 10k lines of rules targeting Aethon-authored chrome (.a2ui-*, .ae-*, .app-*) for the sidebar, header, tab strip, terminal panel, palette, context menus, and overlays. Consumes shape tokens from tokens.css and color tokens from themes.css via CSS custom properties.
nodes.languageNotes: Token-driven CSS: every visual value resolves through var(--…) so palettes and shape vocabulary stay swappable. Enforces an app-like text-selection policy (user-select: none on chrome containers, opt-in via data-selectable).

nodes.name: themes.css
nodes.summary: Defines the seven ship-ready theme palettes (ember, paper, aether/signature, brink, daylight, mist, nocturne) as per-theme color and state tokens scoped under :root[data-theme="<id>"]. The App sets data-theme on <html> and extensions register new palettes via aethon.registerTheme.
nodes.languageNotes: Each theme block overrides the same canonical token set (surfaces, borders, text, accents, state colors) so a single data-theme attribute swap restyles the whole chrome layer without touching component selectors.

nodes.name: tokens.css
nodes.summary: Theme-agnostic design-token vocabulary defining typography (scale + semantic roles), spacing, density, radius, z-index, opacity, easing, motion timing, focus ring, elevation, and app-level layout custom properties on :root. Color tokens live separately in themes.css; chrome rules consume both.
nodes.languageNotes: Semantic typography roles (display/title/body/caption) compose from a raw --text-* scale so themes can re-tune type personality without rewriting components. Extensions can override individual tokens globally or per-theme.

nodes.name: agent-tabs.md
nodes.summary: Guide page explaining agent tabs in Aethon — their anatomy, tab management, per-tab state, message composing/queuing, model switching, and tool execution.

nodes.name: agents.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide to the agent runtime covering models/providers, multi-account auth profiles, subagents with frontmatter, system-prompt customization, and agent-driven UI customization.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest page in the batch (497 lines, 27 sections) with deep coverage of auth-profile and subagent semantics.

nodes.name: command-palette.md
nodes.summary: Guide page documenting the command palette's three modes, query prefixes, keyboard navigation, palette contents, and how extensions can replace it.

nodes.name: configuration.md
nodes.summary: Configuration reference walking through the config.toml file layout, the .aethon directory, and recipes for themes, timeouts, devshell wrap, notifications, voice input, team guardrails, and per-project overrides.
nodes.languageNotes: Recipe-style guide (19 sections) mapping each config.toml key to a practical task.

nodes.name: extensions.md
nodes.summary: Guide to Aethon extensions — what they can register, the three install paths (user-level, npm-distributed, project-local), lifecycle/feedback, and authoring basics.

nodes.name: installation.md
nodes.summary: Installation guide covering release downloads, the Nix install path, running from source for contributors, first-run provider-key setup, and verification.

nodes.name: layouts.md
nodes.summary: Guide explaining Aethon's layout-as-payload model — the built-in workstation layout, the canonical slot contract, switching/registering custom layouts, and resetting.

nodes.name: projects.md
nodes.summary: Guide to projects in Aethon — why they matter, adding/switching/removing projects, active-vs-tab project distinction, project-local extensions, workspaces, and per-project pi sessions.

nodes.name: quick-start.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step quick-start walking a new user through opening a project, sending a first message, switching models, opening shells, using tabs and layouts, and discovering extensions.

nodes.name: settings-and-search.md
nodes.summary: Guide covering the settings panel (Cmd+,), cross-session search (Cmd+Shift+F), the notification stack, and how extensions can replace these surfaces.

nodes.name: shells-and-share-modes.md
nodes.summary: Guide to the terminal panel and its security model — why shells live in a separate panel, their capabilities, share modes as the privacy boundary, the Allow/Deny prompt, and configuring defaults.

nodes.name: themes.md
nodes.summary: Guide to theming — the seven built-in themes, how themes resolve to CSS variables, registering custom themes via drop-in JSON or extensions, required and polished-theme variables, and WCAG-AA contrast.

nodes.name: index.md
nodes.summary: VitePress home page for the Aethon documentation site, with a hero banner, four feature cards (agent-rendered UI, tabs/PTY shells, opt-in shell sharing, themes/extensions), and links into the installation and quick-start guides.
nodes.languageNotes: VitePress home layout driven by YAML frontmatter (layout: home, hero, features) with inline HTML/CSS for the marketing sections.

nodes.name: package.json

layers.name: Rust/Tauri Shell
layers.description: The thin Tauri 2 native backend (src-tauri/src) that owns OS boundaries: IPC commands, the agent-process supervisor, PTY shell tabs, window-state persistence, devshell resolution, voice capture, mDNS discovery, and auto-update probation.

layers.name: Agent Bridge
layers.description: The TypeScript pi-coding-agent subprocess (agent/) that speaks JSON-lines over stdio: dispatcher, agent state, system-prompt composition, auth profiles, subagents, devshell client, and the globalThis.aethon API exposed to tools and extensions.

layers.name: Frontend UI & A2UI Layout
layers.description: The React rendering surface (src/components, src/extensions): the A2UIRenderer, primitive component registry, default-layout payload (workstation.a2ui.json + slots), Monaco editor, file icons, and styles that paint the chrome.

layers.name: Frontend Runtime & State
layers.description: The wiring of the React shell (src/hooks, eventRoutes, state, runtime, services, app): the single JSON-Pointer state store, bridge-message handlers, event routing, OS-edge subscribers, overlays, and the window.aethon runtime API.

layers.name: Shared Utilities & Types
layers.description: Cross-cutting frontend helpers and type definitions (src/utils, src/types): JSON Pointer / data-binding utilities, platform detection, and shared TypeScript contracts imported across the UI and runtime layers.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Colocated Vitest unit specs across the frontend, agent bridge, and CLI plus the Playwright e2e suite — every *.test.* / *.spec.* file that exercises the three code layers.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation and the VitePress docs site (website/, docs/, *.md): user guides, runtime/config references, the bundled agent docs, SPEC/DESIGN/RELEASING, and AGENTS/CLAUDE instructions.

layers.name: CLI, Scripts & Examples
layers.description: Developer-facing tooling outside the app: the aethonctl control CLI, build/dev/release shell scripts, example pi-extensions, e2e harness support, and Claude/Impeccable skill scripts.

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Deployment and continuous-integration assets: GitHub Actions workflows and their steps, the AUR packaging (PKGBUILD/.SRCINFO), the build Dockerfile, and .dockerignore.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Build and project configuration: TypeScript project files, Vite/ESLint/Playwright configs, the Nix flake, Cargo/Tauri manifests, release-please config, capabilities, and entry index.html.

tour.title: What Is Aethon?
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the core idea: Aethon is "pi with a face" — a Tauri 2 desktop shell that embeds the pi coding agent and renders its output as live, interactive UI. The key mental model is layout-as-payload: the interface is not a fixed IDE but a canvas the agent populates dynamically over the A2UI protocol. The README also lays out the three deliberate layers (Rust/Tauri shell, TypeScript agent bridge, React frontend) that this whole tour walks through. SPEC.md adds the milestone-by-milestone vision and the rationale behind the layered architecture.

tour.title: Product & Design Intent
tour.description: Before reading code, absorb the why. PRODUCT.md describes the target user, brand personality, and design principles, while DESIGN.md is the concrete design system — the Ember Brass palette, typography hierarchy, elevation vocabulary, and the signature agent-canvas and terminal surfaces. These documents explain the aesthetic and UX decisions you will see baked into the default layout and components later in the tour.

tour.title: Frontend Boot
tour.description: The React frontend boots from src/main.tsx, which applies the saved theme as early as possible before mounting the app, then hands off to src/App.tsx. App.tsx is the root shell — but notice it is intentionally thin: it composes roughly forty useX() hooks, bootstraps the single state store, wires OS-edge listeners and native window sync, and renders the A2UI workstation layout. This is the "how does it start?" answer for the layer the user actually sees.
tour.languageLesson: React composition: rather than packing logic into one giant component, App.tsx delegates to many small custom hooks (useX()). Each hook owns one concern and returns state plus handlers, keeping the root render almost declarative.

tour.title: Layout As A Payload
tour.description: This is the single most important idea in the codebase. The default UI is not hardcoded React — it is workstation.a2ui.json, a data document describing the entire app chrome (header, sidebar, canvas, composer, terminal, status) and the initial state store. slots.json defines the canonical slot contract that any layout must satisfy. Because the layout is data, the agent can mutate it at runtime, which is exactly what makes Aethon "the agent decides what you see."

tour.title: The A2UI Renderer & State Model
tour.description: The A2UIRenderer is the engine that turns those layout payloads (from Step 4 and from agent output) into a live React tree. It wires the 20 built-in primitives plus extension components, handles for-each templating, and applies optimistic $ref state updates. All app state lives in one object, addressed by JSON Pointer: jsonPointer.ts resolves/sets/deletes by pointer with structural-sharing clones, and dataBinding.ts turns a component's {"$ref": "/path"} props into concrete values. Together these three files are how data becomes pixels and how user input flows back into state.
tour.languageLesson: JSON-Pointer optimistic updates: on a change/submit event bound to a $ref input, the renderer immediately writes the new value back to that pointer path in the state store (a structural-sharing clone, so React re-renders only the changed subtree) before the round-trip to the backend confirms it — the UI feels instant.

tour.title: Shared Types & Two Registries
tour.description: These types are the vocabulary the whole frontend speaks — and the fan-in ranking confirms it: tab.ts and a2ui.ts are the two most depended-upon files in the project (144 and 100 inbound edges). a2ui.ts defines the A2UI payload protocol (components, slots, messages, $ref bindings, the app state shape); tab.ts defines the Tab type and pure helpers for bucketing and active-tab resolution. The ExtensionRegistry is the override surface: extensions register their own component/template implementations to swap chrome composites at runtime, which is why new chrome never gets hardcoded into App.tsx.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript discriminated unions: the A2UI component and message types use a literal "type" field as a discriminant, so the renderer can switch on it and TypeScript narrows each branch to the correct prop shape — a compile-time guarantee that every component kind is handled.

tour.title: Default Layout & Event Routing
tour.description: The default-layout extension entry point assembles the slot catalogue and registers all the chrome components, panels, themes, and keybindings that make up the workstation — it is the concrete implementation behind the JSON from Step 4. When a user clicks or types, eventRoutes/index.ts is the central dispatcher: it resolves each A2UI event through shell-consent prefixes, extension-registered routes, then id/type-keyed built-in handlers, in that precedence order. Returning true marks an event handled and suppresses the default forward to the backend.

tour.title: Crossing The Bridge: Frontend <-> Agent
tour.description: Now we leave the React layer. useBridgeMessages wires the agent-response Tauri event into a batched payload pump that drives the state store, and bridgeMessageHandlers/index.ts is the registry mapping every inbound bridge message type to its handler. This is the frontend half of the JSON-lines conversation with the agent: agent output arrives as messages here, gets applied to the single state store, and the A2UIRenderer (Step 5) re-renders. This is the seam where "the agent mutates the UI" actually happens.

tour.title: The Agent Bridge
tour.description: agent/main.ts is the bridge entry point (the top code entry point by score): it builds the AethonAgentState, loads auth profiles, extensions, themes and subagents, and restores the default tab's session. dispatcher.ts is the JSON-lines reader loop that routes each inbound stdin message to a handler — the mirror image of Step 8 on the agent side. state.ts is the AethonAgentState class holding all bridge runtime state (tabs, extensions, themes, mutation tracking, the frontend-ready handshake); it is the third-most depended-upon file in the project, the agent-side counterpart to the frontend's shared types.
tour.languageLesson: JSON-lines over stdio: the bridge and shell exchange one JSON object per newline-terminated line on the child process's stdin/stdout. It needs no framing protocol or HTTP server — readline yields complete messages, and either side can stream events independently.

tour.title: globalThis.aethon: The Agent's UI API
tour.description: aethon-api.ts assembles the full globalThis.aethon runtime that pi tools and extensions use to mutate the UI — composing layout, canvas, sessions, shells, windows, dashboard, editor, keybindings, themes, and notifications. The clustering analysis surfaced its tightest neighborhood: aethon-api.ts, state.ts, and mutation-ack.ts move together as a unit. mutation-ack.ts is the round-trip acknowledgement primitive — it mints tracked mutation IDs so a UI change initiated by the agent can be confirmed (or rejected) by the frontend, which is how background workers are stopped from clobbering the active workspace's components.
tour.languageLesson: Origin-gating: registry-replacing messages (components, themes, layouts) are stamped with the worker's originTabId so the frontend can reject hydrates from a background workspace's worker. Identity-on-the-message, validated at the receiver, is how multi-bridge concurrency stays safe.

tour.title: The Rust/Tauri Shell & Agent Supervisor
tour.description: lib.rs is the thin Tauri entry point: its run() builder registers plugins, managed state, every IPC command handler, and the setup hooks (window restore, server boot, file watchers). commands/mod.rs is the barrel re-exporting the whole IPC surface (boot, config, devshell, fs, git, session, updater, voice, window, and more). The agent supervisor in agent_process/ is what actually spawns the bun bridge from Steps 9-10: spawn.rs launches the child with the right env, and readers.rs runs the stdout/stderr reader threads that emit the agent-response events the frontend consumes in Step 8.

tour.title: PTY Shells & The Nix Devshell Wrap
tour.description: Beyond the agent, the Rust shell hosts interactive terminals. shell/lifecycle/open.rs spawns portable-pty shell tabs and applies the resolved Nix devshell env so a shell opened inside a flake project is already wrapped. sharemode.rs is the security boundary — the ShareMode enum and transition logic that enforce per-tab opt-in before an agent can read or write a shell. devshell/resolve.rs is the resolver that runs nix print-dev-env / direnv exec env to compute that environment, feeding both the PTY shells and the agent's bash tool from one source of truth.
tour.languageLesson: UTF-8 carry buffer: the PTY reader cannot assume each OS read ends on a character boundary, so it keeps a carry buffer and uses Utf8Error::error_len() to split off any partial multi-byte sequence and prepend it to the next read. Per-chunk from_utf8_lossy would corrupt split emoji and CJK characters.

tour.title: Supporting Subsystems
tour.description: Aethon's Rust shell carries several self-contained subsystems that round out the desktop experience. window_state/restore.rs persists and restores window geometry in logical units before the window becomes visible, so the app reopens exactly where you left it. updater.rs drives channel-aware auto-updates (stable/nightly) with GitHub-API discovery and a pending-update stash. voice/mod.rs is the voice-to-text core that orchestrates a local Whisper model or native OS recognizers behind a common engine trait. server/mod.rs is the mDNS discovery scaffold that advertises and browses for peer Aethon instances on the LAN.

tour.title: Build Config & CI Capstone
tour.description: The tour closes with how Aethon is built, configured, and shipped. flake.nix is the reproducible Nix dev/build environment (Rust pinned to 1.92, plus the platform-specific WebKit/GTK and macOS linker workarounds); tauri.conf.json sets product identity, build hooks, and the overlay-titlebar window; package.json declares the React 19 + pi-coding-agent + Vite/Vitest stack and the dev/build/test scripts. The CI pipeline is the safety net: ci.yml fires on every push/PR and delegates to the reusable _ci.yml, the single source of truth for the lint + typecheck + Rust/TS test + Playwright e2e gate that every change must pass.
tour.languageLesson: Reusable GitHub Actions workflows: ci.yml is a thin trigger that calls _ci.yml via workflow_call, so the same lint/typecheck/test job definition is consumed by both push/PR CI and the release-please pipeline — one source of truth, no copy-pasted job matrices.
